title: Static page generation on >= 12.0.0: document is not defined in styled-jsx
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

N/A

### What operating system are you using?

Linux

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

When running next build on an existing working project being upgraded from Next.js 11 to Next.js >= 12.0.0, the build ends by static page generation failing with the following error:

```
Error occurred prerendering page "/". Read more: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/prerender-error

ReferenceError: document is not defined
    at new StyleSheet (/project-dir/node_modules/styled-jsx/dist/lib/stylesheet.js:40:35)
    at new StyleSheetRegistry (/project-dir/node_modules/styled-jsx/dist/stylesheet-registry.js:51:33)
    at Object.createStyleRegistry (/project-dir/node_modules/styled-jsx/dist/stylesheet-registry.js:227:10)
    at renderToHTML (/project-dir/node_modules/next/dist/server/render.js:292:46)
    at async /project-dir/node_modules/next/dist/export/worker.js:273:36
    at async Span.traceAsyncFn (/project-dir/node_modules/next/dist/trace/trace.js:74:20)

Error occurred prerendering page "/advertise". Read more: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/prerender-error

ReferenceError: document is not defined
    at new StyleSheet (/project-dir/node_modules/styled-jsx/dist/lib/stylesheet.js:40:35)
    at new StyleSheetRegistry (/project-dir/node_modules/styled-jsx/dist/stylesheet-registry.js:51:33)
    at Object.createStyleRegistry (/project-dir/node_modules/styled-jsx/dist/stylesheet-registry.js:227:10)
    at renderToHTML (/project-dir/node_modules/next/dist/server/render.js:292:46)
    at async /project-dir/node_modules/next/dist/export/worker.js:273:36
    at async Span.traceAsyncFn (/project-dir/node_modules/next/dist/trace/trace.js:74:20)

...

Generating static pages (123/123)

> Build error occurred
Error: Export encountered errors on following paths:
        /
        /404
        /_error: /500
        /advertise
        ...
```

And every static page fails to build. The weird thing is /_error is listed even though I don't even have a custom error page, and my 404 page is super basic with just an image in a div and some text.

I've removed all css-modifying plugins from next.config.js and I've tried building with a barebones next.config.js to no avail. I've also tried commenting out all global css imports in _app.js to no avail. As far as I knew, I wasn't even using styled-jsx; the closest thing I'm doing is inline styles in some components.

Has anyone seen anything like this? I'm absolutely stumped and it's the only reason I haven't upgraded to v12 yet.

### Expected Behavior

Static pages should build successfully without styled-jsx errors.

### To Reproduce

This is a tough one since I'm not exactly sure what is causing it. On some pages I'm using Material UI v4 and antd, and I thought one of these might be the culprit, but commenting out all relevant imports the issue remains, and it's not even used on some of the more basic static pages this is happening on, so I'm stumped.
author: goguda

title: outputStandalone breaking with middleware
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8-canary.13

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

When using `experimental.outputStandalone` with middlewares, `yarn build` is breaking. since nft.json file is not generated for middleware. 

```bash
[Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '~/next.js/examples/with-docker/.next/server/pages/_middleware.js.nft.json'] {
```

### Expected Behavior

`yarn build` to succeed and tracing files for middleware to be generated. 

### To Reproduce

create `pages/_middleware.js` in [with-docker](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/c87204e/examples/with-docker) example and then `yarn build` is breaking


```js
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'

export function middleware(req, ev) {
  return NextResponse.next()
}
```

title: Issue when deploying to Google Cloud Run
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.2

### What version of Node.js are you using?

12.17

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Windows

### How are you deploying your application?

Google Cloud

### Describe the Bug

When I build my application for deployment with Google Cloud Build, I receive no errors and my Docker image builds correctly. However, when I go to deploy the image with Google Cloud Run, I receive the error
```Error: Cannot find module '/app/.next/server/middleware-manifest.json'```
which is strange,  considering that I didn't create any middleware for my application. 


### Expected Behavior

The application should run correctly with no issue.

### To Reproduce

Dockerfile:
```
# Install dependencies only when needed
FROM node:14.17-alpine AS deps
# Check https://github.com/nodejs/docker-node/tree/b4117f9333da4138b03a546ec926ef50a31506c3#nodealpine to understand why libc6-compat might be needed.
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# Rebuild the source code only when needed
FROM node:14.17-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV=production
###########################################
# There are other ENV variables here that I have removed 
###########################################
COPY . .
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
RUN npm run build && npm install

# Production image, copy all the files and run next
FROM node:14.17-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV production

RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs
RUN adduser -S nextjs -u 1001

# You only need to copy next.config.js if you are NOT using the default configuration
COPY --from=builder /app/next.config.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/next-i18next.config.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/htmlserializer.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/tailwind.config.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/firebaseConfig.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/postcss.config.js ./
COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next ./.next
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json

USER nextjs

EXPOSE 3000

ENV PORT 3000

# Next.js collects completely anonymous telemetry data about general usage.
# Learn more here: https://nextjs.org/telemetry
# Uncomment the following line in case you want to disable telemetry.
# ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED 1

CMD ["node_modules/.bin/next", "start"]
```

cloud-build.yaml

```
steps:
  - name: 'gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker'
    entrypoint: 'bash'
    args:
      - '-c'
      - |
        docker pull gcr.io/[MY PROJECT ID]/website:latest || exit 0
  - name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    timeout: 1200s
    args:
      [
        'build',
        '-f',
        'Dockerfile',
        '-t',
        'gcr.io/[MY PROJECT ID]/website',
        '--cache-from',
        'gcr.io/[MY PROJECT ID]/website:latest',
        '.',
      ]

timeout: 1200s
images:
  - gcr.io/[MY PROJECT ID]/website
```

body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

NA

### Describe the Bug

In `next.config.js`:
- With `target` set to `"serverless"`, `next build` fails with a `@swc/core` error

```console
Λ  npm run build

> build
> next build

warn  - The `target` config is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
See more info here https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/deprecated-target-config

Failed to compile.

./node_modules/@swc/core-darwin-arm64/swc.darwin-arm64.node
Module parse failed: Unexpected character '�' (1:0)
You may need an appropriate loader to handle this file type, currently no loaders are configured to process this file. See https://webpack.js.org/concepts#loaders
(Source code omitted for this binary file)

Import trace for requested module:
./node_modules/@swc/core/binding.js
./node_modules/@swc/core/index.js
./pages/index.js
./node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/next-serverless-loader/index.js?page=%2F&absolutePagePath=private-next-pages%2Findex.js&absoluteAppPath=private-next-pages%2F_app.js&absoluteDocumentPath=next%2Fdist%2Fpages%2F_document&absoluteErrorPath=next%2Fdist%2Fpages%2F_error&absolute404Path=&distDir=private-dot-next&buildId=JmVNe6U-5I794-fq-99iR&assetPrefix=&generateEtags=true&poweredByHeader=true&canonicalBase=&basePath=&runtimeConfig=&previewProps=%7B%22previewModeId%22%3A%227c966c6ac5bd418e1821d433e6fed70b%22%2C%22previewModeSigningKey%22%3A%22dc1fb0e2675a03a36e8fafc41b15dfe5abc180f20875f3ad8319f65efafbe541%22%2C%22previewModeEncryptionKey%22%3A%224759ddbc51d347fe23a07d713717aeeb1c4fe00e032bf2d550a7f4bacc74aafc%22%7D&loadedEnvFiles=W10%3D&i18n=!

title: Image optimization URL doesn't work when images introduced to file system after nextJS server started
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.3.0

### What browser are you using?

all

### What operating system are you using?

linux

### How are you deploying your application?

yarn dev / yarn build && yarn start

### Describe the Bug

NextJS response with optimized images that existed on the file system BEFORE I started the server. If I upload an image and place it in the `public/uploads/foobar.jpg` path when I attempt to display that image on the page it just hangs and doesn't render anything. If I restart the server and access the URL the image is rendered as expected.

I noticed when I try to access an image that doesn't exist the connection hangs indefinitely.

I also noticed that when if I'm running the build version (not dev) of nextjs and I introduce a file after the server is running and attempt to access it I get this error in the console:

```
TypeError: res.hasHeader is not a function
    at setRevalidateHeaders (/path/to/app/node_modules/next/dist/server/send-payload.js:18:37)
    at Object.sendRenderResult (/path/to/app/node_modules/next/dist/server/send-payload.js:51:9)
```


### Expected Behavior

Expect images to be rendered that get uploaded after the next server is already running. Also when an image is missing to respond with 404Expect images to be rendered that get uploaded after the next server is already running. Also when an image is missing to respond with 404

### To Reproduce

1. start next server
2. Upload image into public/uploads/abc.jpg
3. access an image optimize URL. eg. http://localhost:3000/_next/image?url=/uploads/abc.jpg&w=640&q=75
4. restart the server and the image will render as expected
author: simontong

title: Be able to enable swc from next.config
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Right now I'm using babel plugins that have no equivalent in swc (transform-react-qa-classes) but I don't need it for development enviroment.
In the future the idea is create a custom swc plugin for that, but now nextjs don't support custom plugins. But for now we would like to be able to enable SWC only in dev.



### Describe the solution you'd like

Currently the only validation of SWC is that there is a babel configuration.
` / packages / next / build / webpack-config.ts # L417`

Could you add one more setting in next.config to force swc?

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Wait for be able to attach custom swc plugins.

title: Shared component (header) gets rerendered on new route
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.4 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 16.13.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? Windows ### How are you deploying your application? Vercel ### Describe the Bug Im trying to share a header component across all pages for my Next.js app but the header gets rerendered when I try to navigate between routes with Next's Link component. I have created a layout component which is supposed to render the header together with all the possible routes. Basically I don't want the header to get rerendered but visiting a different route using Next.js router it gets rerendered for some reason. _app.tsx ``` function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }: AppProps) { return } ``` LayoutMain.tsx (This is where I the ```` is provided from _app.tsx is supposed to get rendered together with the Header) ``` function LayoutMain({children}: {children: React.ReactNode}){ const {user} = useContext(AuthContext) if(!user) return return <>
{children} } ``` Header.tsx ``` function Header() { console.log('Header rendered') const {user} = useContext(AuthContext) return
} ``` ### Expected Behavior Shared component to not rerender when a new route is visited. ### To Reproduce _app.tsx ``` function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }: AppProps) { return } ``` LayoutMain.tsx (This is where I the ```` is provided from _app.tsx is supposed to get rendered together with the Header) ``` function LayoutMain({children}: {children: React.ReactNode}){ const {user} = useContext(AuthContext) if(!user) return return <>
{children} } ``` Header.tsx ``` function Header() { console.log('Header rendered') const {user} = useContext(AuthContext) return
} ```
author: geoli96

title: Deploying nextjs on multiple instance result in different build files on each server
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

10.1.2

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.16.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Linux

### How are you deploying your application?

next build && next start -p $PORT on AWS EBS

### Describe the Bug

I use AWS Elasticbeanstalk to host my application behind a load balancer. When I scale my application from 1 instance to 2 instance, the build files generated by next are not the same in both instance, resulting in 404 errors:

This file only exist on one of the instance:
https://example.com/_next/static/chunks/pages/personal-information-0cc9b5545e1107d69f10.js

the other instance has this file:
https://example.com/_next/static/chunks/pages/personal-information-f73dfe8ac657b9e7818b.js

Notice the value is different after `personal-information-`

This inconsistency in the filename results in errors in my browser such as:

```
Request URL: https://example.com/_next/static/chunks/pages/personal-information-0cc9b5545e1107d69f10.js
Request Method: GET
Status Code: 404
```

Should the file name be the same if nothing has changed? This current setup is problematic when running multiple instances for the same application.

### Expected Behavior

The file names should be the same if no code has changed

### To Reproduce

Deploy a next js app on 2 instance, here's my script in package.js:
```
 ...
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next build && next start -p $PORT"
  },
  ...
  ```

title: Dynamic Routes take precedence over API routes
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.3

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.9.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

I have a nearly completely generated Next.js page, that is wired to a headless CMS.
So I basically only have a single page

```
drwxr-xr-x    - jannikzs  4 Jan 11:48 .
.rw-r--r-- 7.1k jannikzs  4 Jan 11:48 ├── [...url].tsx
.rw-r--r--  878 jannikzs 19 Dec  2021 ├── _app.tsx
.rw-r--r--  447 jannikzs 22 Nov  2021 ├── _document.tsx
drwxr-xr-x    - jannikzs  4 Jan 11:48 ├── api
.rw-r--r--  324 jannikzs  4 Jan 11:48 │  ├── exit-preview.ts
.rw-r--r--  921 jannikzs  4 Jan 11:32 │  └── preview.ts
.rw-r--r-- 1.0k jannikzs 19 Dec  2021 └── index.tsx
```

and some API routes for the preview mode.

When I now call the `/api/exit-preview` URL, it will try to generate the oage `[..url]` for that instead of just accessing the API route.
How can I give the API route precedence?

Also I noticed that when nesting the preview exit handler in `/api/preview/exit.tsx` it just doesn't work, I don't get the next.js specific request and response objects, but just generic ones?
At least `res.clearPreviewData()` wasn't defined.

### Expected Behavior

All statically defined pages / API routes should take precedence over dynamic pages.

### To Reproduce

See the setup above, it should be fairly easy to reproduce.

title: Unexpected behavior when getServerSideProps is defined but is set to undefined
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) _No response_ ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.7 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 16.3.0 ### What browser are you using? Firefox ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? next start ### Describe the Bug When getServerSideProps is defined but is set to undefined next returns html on data request. Undefined is obviously invalid value but it is easy to happen if the page uses for example a higher-order function to wrap the getServerSideProps and it can be very confusing to find the cause. ### Expected Behavior Next should return either the json data or throw error or warning that getServerSideProps is not valid. ### To Reproduce ```jsx // index.js const Page = () => { return
Page
; } export default Page; ``` ```jsx // page.js export const getServerSideProps = undefined; const Page = () => { return
page
; } export default Page; ``` If user clicks on the link, next tries to fetch data and `/_next/data/development/page.json` returns html.
author: AlfonzAlfonz

title: Incorrect symlinks when using experimental.outputFileTracingRoot
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Other

### Describe the Bug

We are trying to use Next.js 12's output file tracing feature https://nextjs.org/blog/next-12#output-file-tracing to speed up building our Docker image and reduce the Docker image size when deploying our application. We are planning to leverage this feature so that we would not have to install dependencies when building our Docker image. However, after enabling `outputStandalone` and using `outputFileTracingRoot` in next.config.js like so:

```js
module.exports = {
  experimental: {
    outputStandalone: true,
    // this includes files from the monorepo base two directories up
    outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '../../'),
  },
}
```

The build output's symlinks seem to have the outputFileTracingRoot path removed from them. 

After digging into the code where the symlinks are created, it looks like it is using the relative path between tracing root and the actual symlink https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/e01ef297e757f0435f82348496e68eb6919826a8/packages/next/build/utils.ts#L1194. For example, if the tracing root is `../..` then it will remove that from the new symlinks in the standalone build output. I think it should use the existing symlink instead of getting the relative path since it is copying over the files from the tracing root.

### Expected Behavior

Symlinks should be kept as is instead of the relative path between the existing symlink and tracing root. 
So instead of 
`await fs.symlink(
              path.relative(tracingRoot, symlink),
              fileOutputPath
            )`
it should be
`await fs.symlink(
              symlink,
              fileOutputPath
            )`

### To Reproduce

1. In a mono repo where the next.js app is one of the packages, update next.config.js to include:
```js
module.exports = {
  experimental: {
    outputStandalone: true,
    // this includes files from the monorepo base two directories up
    outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname, '../../'),
  },
}
```
2. build the app using `next build`
3. in the console, navigate to the .next directory for the standalone build output and find a symlink in the app package's node_modules
4. use `ls -l <symlink>` to view the target path
5. notice that the target path is not pointing to the actual file location but is missing the relative path to the tracing root.

title: Middleware x Rewriting url without locale prefix returns 404
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v14.15.4

### What browser are you using?

chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

vercel

### Describe the Bug

While rewriting subdomain in middleware, page is not loaded based on locale prefix. 

```
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(req, ev) {
    ...

    // rewrite
    if (
        !pathname.includes('.') && // exclude all files in the public folder
        !pathname.startsWith('/api') // exclude all API routes
    ) {
        return NextResponse.rewrite(`/a/${subdomain}`); // return 404 not found
    }

    return response;
}

```

I tried `return NextResponse.rewrite("/en/a/support")` instead. In this case, the first hit returns white screen. second hit returns proper page.

Note that `/a/support` page use `getStaticProps` and `getStaticPaths` with `fallback=true`.

Sharing next.config.js
```
const moduleExports = {
    ...
    i18n: {
        locales: ['en', 'id', 'es'],
        defaultLocale: 'en'
    }
    ...
}
```



### Expected Behavior

return NextResponse.rewrite(`/en/a/${subdomain}`) should work 

### To Reproduce

`i18n` setting in next.config.js

prepare ISR page 

In middleware, rewrite without locale preifx. e.g. `return NextResponse.rewrite(`/a/abc`)`

body:

Chrome

macOS

next start

### Describe the Bug

When I add a local image with the next/image component without specify width and height according to [https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/image-optimization#local-images](url) I always have the error:
`Image with src "static/media/public/images/x.png" must use "width" and "height" properties or "layout='fill'" property.` on Storybook.

I don't have any problem outside Storybook.

### Expected Behavior

I would like not to add, just for Storybook, width and height for local image.

### To Reproduce

- Use the example `with-storybook` [https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-storybook](url)
- In `public` directory, add an image 
- In `index.js`, import this image and use the `next/image` component with only `src`, according to [https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/image-optimization#local-images](url)
- In `.storybook/main.js`, add `staticDirs: ["../public"]`
- In `.storybook/preview.js`, add
```
import * as NextImage from "next/image";

const OriginalNextImage = NextImage.default;

Object.defineProperty(NextImage, "default", {
  configurable: true,
  value: (props) => <OriginalNextImage {...props} unoptimized />,
});
```
- Launch Storybook

title: Can't transpile a babel macro project
body: ### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) `next info` is still not available, but I run `npx envinfo --binaries --system`: ``` System: OS: macOS 12.0.1 CPU: (10) arm64 Apple M1 Pro Memory: 76.88 MB / 32.00 GB Shell: 3.3.1 - /opt/homebrew/bin/fish Binaries: Node: 16.13.1 - ~/.local/share/nvm/v16.13.1/bin/node Yarn: 1.22.17 - /opt/homebrew/bin/yarn npm: 8.1.2 - ~/.local/share/nvm/v16.13.1/bin/npm ``` ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.8-canary.16 ### What version of Node.js are you using? v16.13.1 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? Vercel ### Describe the Bug For some reason, Next.js can't transpile `.macro` files. The terminal is reporting something related to an `uid`, looks like an internal thing: ``` ready - started server on 0.0.0.0:3000, url: http://localhost:3000 info - Disabled SWC as replacement for Babel because of custom Babel configuration ".babelrc" https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/swc-disabled event - compiled client and server successfully in 152 ms (144 modules) wait - compiling / (client and server)... info - Using external babel configuration from /Users/kikobeats/Downloads/with-babel-macros-app/.babelrc error - ./pages/index.js TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'uid') (node:13611) [DEP_WEBPACK_MODULE_ISSUER] DeprecationWarning: Module.issuer: Use new ModuleGraph API (Use `node --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created) wait - compiling /_error (client and server)... error - ./pages/index.js TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'uid') ``` I think what is happening is since the macro file is called `*.macro.js`, Next.js is wrongly interpreting it instead of leveraging to babel-plugin-macros for doing the job. ### Expected Behavior The macro should be interpreted by the babel plugin, not being interfered with by the Next.js internal compiler. ### To Reproduce just clone [with-babel-macros](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-babel-macros)

title: `next/jest` sets `NODE_ENV` to `'production'`
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: arm64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.1.0: Wed Oct 13 17:33:24 PDT 2021; root:xnu-8019.41.5~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T8101
    Binaries:
      Node: 14.17.6
      npm: 8.1.4
      Yarn: 1.22.15
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8-canary.17
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8-canary.17

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.17.6

### What browser are you using?

N/A

### What operating system are you using?

macOS 12.0.1 (21A559)

### How are you deploying your application?

N/A

### Describe the Bug

The `next/jest` preset sets `NODE_ENV` to `'production'`.

Users familiar with Jest will expect `NODE_ENV` to be `'test'` when running tests. The documentation says:
> Set to `'test'` if it's not already set to something else.

In this case, `NODE_ENV` is indeed set to `'test'` initially, but is replaced with `'production'`. I've verified this simply by console logging the environment in the test setup files, and again in a test. I did take a _quick_ look, but wasn't able to identify the cause.

This is related to #17032 and #17903.

### Expected Behavior

When running tests in Jest, `NODE_ENV` should be `'test'`.

### To Reproduce

Create a new app using the Jest example:
```sh
npx create-next-app --example with-jest with-jest-app
```

Add the following line to the example test:
```ts
// __tests__/index.test.tsx
console.log(process.env.NODE_ENV); // production
```

Run:
```sh
npm test
```
author: mrmckeb

title: segfault running custom next server within jest unit test after upgrading nextjs 11 -> 12
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

N/A

### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.3 + 12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

N/A

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

N/A

### Describe the Bug

Within a custom server application there are some unit tests using the jest framework along with supertest to mimic http requests [(See replication repo)](https://github.com/Kauabunga/nextjs-replicate-segfault-custom-server-v12). This has been working as expected up until trying to upgrade to multiple 12.X nextjs versions including `12.0.0`, `12.0.1`, and `12.0.7`.

When running version 12 with a `next.config.js` configuration present and a custom server with `dev: false`, the jest execution exits with a segfault when calling `app.prepare()`.


```javascript
const app = next({
      dev: false,
});

await app.prepare(); // Goes 💥
```

```sh
zsh: segmentation fault  npm test
```

It is possible to workaround this issue by importing the nextjs config manually and passing it as the `conf` option to the custom next application.

example workaround

```javascript
const app = next({
      dev: false,
      conf: require('./next.config'), // this fixes the seg fault
});

await app.prepare(); // No 💥 this time

await request(app.getRequestHandler())
      .get("/")
      .expect(200);
```

### Expected Behavior

I would expect this not to crash and nextjs to be able to resolve the `next.config.js` automatically without the conf option

### To Reproduce

I have published a public GitHub repo that reproduces the following scenarios along with setup instructions

[https://github.com/Kauabunga/nextjs-replicate-segfault-custom-server-v12](https://github.com/Kauabunga/nextjs-replicate-segfault-custom-server-v12)


1. [✓] jest unit test with version 11.1.3 without a `next.config.js`
2. [✓] jest unit test with version 11.1.3 with a `next.config.js`
3. [✓] jest unit test with version 12.0.7 without a `next.config.js`
4. [✗] jest unit test with version 12.0.7 with a `next.config.js`
5. [✓] jest unit test with version 12.0.7 with a `next.config.js` with custom server options `conf: require('next.config')`
6. [✗] jest unit test with version canary with a `next.config.js` and `segfault-handler`
author: Kauabunga

title: [Feature Request] Add an programmatic API to share context between prerender workers during `getStaticPaths` and `getStaticProps`
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

RT.

Context: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/32981

In short, I am using a node library at local as my backend CMS. Unlike requesting a remote GraphQL endpoint or querying a database (which is damn slow), it is *FAAAAASSSSST enough* that I need just one instance. With the Next.js multi-thread feature when prerendering the static page, starting multiple instances of the library actually makes the build slower.

**Related context**

- The content of Next.js worker where every component is individual: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/export/worker.ts
- Where Next.js starts multiple instances of the pretender worker:
https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/cfa8ab9cbfb8818408654e223aa9253be619879e/packages/next/build/index.ts#L780-L791

### Describe the solution you'd like

Allow `getStaticProps` and `getStaticPaths` to have a shared context where I can put the local node library CMS in.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

An option to disable the multi-thread feature completely.

title: Can not use React 18 feature - useSyncExternalStore, maybe updating the pre-compiled dependency react-server-dom-webpack will help
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8-canary.17

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next dev

### Describe the Bug

Testing a Recoil version (Nightly, 0.6 probably released shortly) in my application, an error occurs that a React 18 hook "useSyncExternalStore" can not be used.

The React version: 18.0.0-rc.0  
NextJS config:
experimental: {
    reactRoot: 'concurrent',
}

Taking a quick search in the NextJs repo, I found NextJs relies on the library react-server-dom-webpack in version 0.0.0-experimental-13455d26d-20211104, which is about two months old. The library gets used in a so-called "compiled" directory; it seems NextJs does not support arbitrary versions and features after all. The following line mentions the hook useSyncExternalStore is unsupported. Does that mean the problem goes await ones NextJs updates to a higher dependency here?

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/1ee13baf000ab391e6017c68eb4770768a8e7674/packages/next/compiled/react-server-dom-webpack/cjs/react-server-dom-webpack-writer.browser.development.server.js#L873

I reference the issue I opened in the Recoil project.
https://github.com/facebookexperimental/Recoil/issues/1526

Thx!

### Expected Behavior

All React features should be supported, especially those shipping with a release candidate.

### To Reproduce

I have created a reproducer; here it is https://github.com/gurkerl83/Recoil-Nighlty-NextJs
It is a tiny NextJs app with only one hook, "useRecoilValue," involved.

Check out the code, then run the following commands

```
yarn => yarn berry gets used, already packaged in the app
yarn dev => to start the development server on localhost:3000
yarn start => optionally, which tries to create a production build but fails due to the error described in this issue.
```

I hope this helps to reproduce; Thx!

title: Using recommended file extension to import TypeScript file causes error "Module not found"
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
$ npx --no-install next --version
Next.js v12.0.7
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

n/a

### What operating system are you using?

Windows

### How are you deploying your application?

n/a

### Describe the Bug

When importing a TypeScript file using a `.js` file extension, Next fails with "Module not found"

```
$ npm run build

> build
> next build

info  - Checking validity of types...
info  - Creating an optimized production build...
Failed to compile.

./pages/index.tsx
Module not found: Can't resolve '../lib/helper.js' in 'C:\Users\james\dev\foobar\pages'


> Build failed because of webpack errors
```

### Expected Behavior

Next.js should resolve TypeScript imports. TypeScript recommends using the `.js` file extension to import TypeScript files:

* See examples at https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/2/modules.html
* https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/40878
* https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/15479#issuecomment-300240856

My Next.js application is not an isolated project; it shares TypeScript modules with other environments that rely on this `.js` file extension.

### To Reproduce

1. `echo 'export const n = 5' > lib/helper.ts`
2. In `pages/index.tsx`, add: `import {n} from '../lib/helper.js'`
3. Run `next build` or `next dev`

title: [NEXT-1178] Remove focus from next/link element after page change
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14

### What browser are you using?

Chrome, Firefox

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

other

### Describe the Bug

In Chrome & Firefox, when changing pages using a next/link the clicked anchor remains focused after page change. This causes anchor styles to remain in their focused state. 

I found this issue - https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/2768  from a few years ago that solved this problem.
This is the PR created by @connor-baer for that issue - https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/3545. The solution was to do `document.body.focus()` on every route change. 

However, the issue appears to be back.

![giiif](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6549140/148380148-03ab07a3-a97d-4275-973e-fe325adaae3b.gif)



### Expected Behavior

When changing pages with a next/link the clicked link should no longer have focus after page change.

### To Reproduce

Visit this in chrome or firefox and click the header links: https://stackblitz.com/edit/nextjs-js-focus-issue

<sub>[NEXT-1178](https://linear.app/vercel/issue/NEXT-1178/remove-focus-from-nextlink-element-after-page-change)</sub>
author: AshConnolly

title: Docs: layout data fetching is using `currently`, does that mean soon it can become possible? Or is this wishful thinking?
body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

On https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/layouts#data-fetching it states: 

```
Inside your layout, you can fetch data on the client-side using useEffect or a library like SWR. 
Because this file is not a Page, you cannot use getStaticProps or getServerSideProps currently.
```

So the word `currently` is used. That can mean that can change, and if so any plans on when? I was hoping to have all my "problems" fixed in 12 :) but cant find a nice way to SSR header and footer data only once. Currently every page loads this data and then it bubbles down.

### Is there any context that might help us understand?

Im just hoping to finally be able to load layout data once and have all Next stuff working (static generating etc etc), still looking at all 12 options but couldn't find it being solved so hence trying to understand if currently can mean it will become possible.

### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/layouts

16.13.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Other platform

### Describe the Bug

I cannot access any runtime variables inside `_middleware.ts` files.

### Expected Behavior

I want to access runtime variables in middlewares.

I couldn't find any documentation about this and hence can only hope that it is a bug and will be fixed. Currently I can only use `process.env.FOO` inside middlewares but that are build time variables, which is not ideal, because I'd prefer to use here server runtime variables.

### To Reproduce

Inside `_middleware.ts`:
```ts
import { NextFetchEvent, NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import getConfig from 'next/config';

// outside the middleware export
console.log(getConfig());

// inside the middleware export (doesn't matter)
export function middleware(request: NextRequest, _event: NextFetchEvent) {
    console.log(getConfig());
    return NextResponse.next();
}
...
```
Always logs `undefined` as any runtime config seems to be not accessible in middlewares.

title: Rule no-unwanted-polyfillio wrongly reports error on es2019 rule
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.0 or 12.0.0

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome 67

### What operating system are you using?

Windows

### How are you deploying your application?

other platform

### Describe the Bug

The eslint rule [no-unwanted-polyfillio](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/errors/no-unwanted-polyfillio.md) wrongly report es2019 as duplicate.

All `esXXX` rule are marked as [Should be covered by babel-preset-env instead.](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/eslint-plugin-next/lib/rules/no-unwanted-polyfillio.js#L52-L59).

My babel configuration is the following:

```js
module.exports = {
  presets: ['next/babel'],
  plugins: ['styled-components'], // See https://github.com/brandonchinn178/next.js/blob/master/examples/with-styled-components/.babelrc
};
```

So I think this is pretty much the default config. I did not customize anything about babel-preset-env

I tried with a boostraped nextjs 12 and swc (without babel configuration) and the issue is the same.

### Expected Behavior

Either do not report as an error, or possibly fix the babel configuration if needed ?

If we only need to fix the eslint rule, I can open a PR. If the fix is on the babel side, I will need some help there.

### To Reproduce

Reproductible repository : https://github.com/jdeniau/nextjs-object-fromentries

Open a chrome <= 71 (or any browser that [does not support `Object.fromEntries`](https://caniuse.com/mdn-javascript_builtins_object_fromentries)) and try to run a nextjs app with the following code.

```js
Object.fromEntries([]);
```

It will crash with an error:
```
Object.fromEntries is not a function
```

title: ReferenceError: fetchData is not defined
body: ### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: x64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64 Binaries: Node: 14.18.2 npm: 6.14.15 Yarn: 1.22.17 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.8-canary.19 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.8-canary.19 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 14.18.2 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? Vercel ### Describe the Bug After upgrading `next` to version 12 i get a `ReferenceError: fetchData is not defined` when using early return without braces inside useEffect, in version 11 it works as expected. Running the `next` command in development works as expected, but `next build` and `next start` the error happens. ```javascript React.useEffect(() => { if (query === '') return function getFetchUrl() { return 'https://hn.algolia.com/api/v1/search?query=' + query } async function fetchData() { const res = await fetch(getFetchUrl()) const data = await res.json() setData(data) } fetchData() }, [query]) ``` ### Expected Behavior Expected to work without the need to add braces. ### To Reproduce Clone this project https://github.com/mjr/next-bug. Run `next build` and then `PORT=3000 next start`. Open `localhost:3000` e open the console to see the error.

author: wsfuller

title: Docs: No info on environment variables in the /src folder
body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

Change this line in [this page](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/src-directory):

> Config files like next.config.js and tsconfig.json should be inside the root directory, moving them to src won't work. Same goes for the public directory

To:

> Config files like next.config.js and tsconfig.json, as well as environment variables, should be inside the root directory, moving them to src won't work. Same goes for the public directory

And add a note [here](https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/environment-variables#loading-environment-variables):

> Note: If you are using a /src folder, please note that Next.JS will load the .env files **only** from the parent folder and **not** from the /src folder.



### Is there any context that might help us understand?

I've had this tiny issue that I didn't think of, but maybe one line in the documentation might help other people as well, since it's a small thing that is [not](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/src-directory) [mentioned](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/environment-variables): if you, by any chance, have environment variables in your /src folder, they're not gonna be read by the program. If you have them in the parent folder however, they will be read. Maybe it's not the most important problem around, but it would be great if it would be mentioned in the documentation somewhere (if not on both the Environment Variables page as well as the /src folder one).

Kind regards

### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/environment-variables#loading-environment-variables
https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/src-directory

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.6.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next dev

### Describe the Bug

I am creating MDX pages with the `@next/mdx` package. These pages have layouts, as described in [the docs](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/using-mdx#layouts).
When changing the layout during development, it gets reloaded once on the first change, but then doesn't reload on subsequent changes. Any changes to the layout component have no effect, even after a page refresh. The changes only come into effect after a dev server restart.


### Expected Behavior

Pages should correctly refresh after layouts update.

### To Reproduce

1. Clone this repository https://github.com/biowaffeln/next-mdx-bug, install dependencies
2. Start the dev server with `yarn dev` and open `localhost:3000`
3. make a change in `components/layout.tsx`. The page should update.
4. make another change in `components/layout.tsx`. The page doesn't update.

title: Image lazyBoundary prop not working with a container different than 'root'
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

7.20.3

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.7.0

### What browser are you using?

firefox

### What operating system are you using?

windows 10

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

Image tag from next/image is using lazy-loading by default, using 'lazyBoundary' prop for adjusting the rootMargin option of the Intersection Observer Api. (defaults  to 200px) . The problem is that this works only in relation to the viewport, meaning that when you have a container other than root with overflow:auto containing the Images, the lazyBoundary is not working correctly since you can not set a prop option similar to "root" of the Intersection Observer Api. So far I have managed to add manually that prop by editing the source code at next\dist\client\use-intersection.js and next\dist\client\image.js and temporarily fix the issue but that of course is not viable.

### Expected Behavior

We need a new prop of the Image component for being able to set the root container for adjusting the lazyBounary (rootMargin) behavior.

### To Reproduce

Create an absolutely positioned container , for example "div" , give overflow:auto, add some Image components inside ,give a large lazyBounary for example 1000px and check that despite the lazyboundary setting the image will only load when it becomes visible.

body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

We have a page that has a bout 10-20K static files generated and we have problems with syncing them to our S3 buckets. 

Our page generates a folder called /learn and has a lot of subsequent  pages stored in it 

```
/lernen/first-of-4k-learn-units
``` 

and my question is, if we can split those in next.js in some way, then deploy them to subdomains and get the client to request it from a subdomain 

example 

```
https://f.content.purgae.com/lernen/first-of-4k-learn-units
```

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like to be able to define a 'splitting pattern' that results in 

* different output folders  by splitting pattern 
* a mechanic in the client to load this from the correct subdomain

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Alternatives considered: 

* SSR (we dont do that)
* removing all static contents out of the page
author: sebsonjura

title: Backward compatible ESM support
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Since Next.js `11.1.3-canary.69`, ESM externals has been turned on by default using the `experimental: { esmExternals: true }` in this [commit](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/29878).

One of the challenges is that the module resolutions between CommonJS and ESM is different. From Node [documentation](https://nodejs.org/api/esm.html):

> The current specifier resolution does not support all default behavior of the CommonJS loader. One of the behavior differences is automatic resolution of file extensions and the ability to import directories that have an index file.

By having this, it means that you could end up with different import paths depending on if you use CommonJS or ESM which will be a really bad developer experience. On top of this, to provide the correct support for Next.js, modules would have to be backward compatible since not all packages support ESM today.

### Describe the solution you'd like

It looks like the way to do would be to support `--experimental-specifier-resolution=node` but from looking at previous issues ([here](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/9607) and [here](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/9487)), it does not seem to work with Next.js (not sure why).

I was not able to get it to work myself, so maybe we could have this as a new experimental option that is supported by Next.js?

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I tried to build a backward compatible package using this [article](https://www.sensedeep.com/blog/posts/2021/how-to-create-single-source-npm-module.html) but ended up with clunky paths which degraded the developer experience.
author: nbouvrette

### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Adopt naming conventions that are more meaningful and inclusive; more specifically, and as the PR title suggests, rename the current `master` branch from `master` to `main`.

- This would require the `canary` to `master` rebase flow to obviously need some configuration change, hopefully this is little effort (in relative terms). 

### Describe the solution you'd like

Refer to above section and this sibling suggestion in the `swr-site` repo that was made and adopted over the weekend, https://github.com/vercel/swr-site/issues/206. 

Additional resources: https://github.com/github/renaming

### Describe alternatives you've considered

The alternative is to leave the current `master` branch name as-is, however, that doesn't seem like a good alternative.

author: azakero

title: How to change limit? API response for ${req.url} exceeds 4MB. This will cause the request to fail in a future version.
body:
### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.0

### How are you deploying your application?

Other platform

### Describe the Bug

When streaming a large file in an API response, are getting this warnings on the terminal.

(this change was first introduced in PR https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/26831 and modified to 4mb in https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/26887)

```
API response for /api/... exceeds 4MB. This will cause the request to fail in a future version. https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/api-routes-body-size-limit
```

But we are not using Vercel. We are not using AWS serverless functions at all. So to throw a warning is not relevant to us.

I am concerned that we will update Next.js and our file downloads will start throwing runtime exceptions in production as wording of #26831

> In a future version this scenario will throw an error.

### Possible solution(s)

I propose a config option to suppress this warning (future error) for non-Vercel users of Next.js.

Alternatively, I'm wondering if middleware would make this a non-issue?

We are hoping to avoid spinning up a custom server, but will do if required.

title: The with-msw installation stops working with React 18 +  experimental concurrency features enabled.
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.07

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Debian host/Docker/Alpine 3.14

### How are you deploying your application?

Debian host/Docker/Alpine container

### Describe the Bug

I want to test out the new React 18 concurrency and server component features using a mock API with msw . I have installed the msw template from [here](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-msw) and all works fine with React 17 and upgraded to React 18 according to instructions [here](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/react-18) 

Prequisite tests: I installed and tested vanilla Next js upgraded to react and react-dom 18 with experimental concurrency configured in next.config.js and it works fine. There appears to be a problem arising between concurrency features and the official next js msw template.

i.e. i used yarn add next@latest react@rc react-dom@rc

after upgrade i have package.json

```
{
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev -p 3201",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start -p 3201"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "msw": "^0.29.0",
    "nanoid": "^3.1.30",
    "next": "^12.0.7",
    "random-words": "^1.1.1",
    "react": "^18.0.0-rc.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.0.0-rc.0"
  },
  "msw": {
    "workerDirectory": "public"
  }
}
```

msw works fine and the github demo works perfectly until I enable concurrent features in next.config.js

```
// next.config.js
module.exports = {
    experimental: {
      concurrentFeatures: true,
    },
}
```

with concurrentFeatures enabled the index.ts doesnt load and I can see an error log on the server:

```
8|next-nex | > next dev -p 3201
8|next-nex |
8|next-nex | ready - started server on 0.0.0.0:3201, url: http://localhost:3201
8|next-nex | info  - Loaded env from /app/next/next18/.env.development
8|next-nex | info  - Using the createRoot API for React
8|next-nex | wait  - compiling...
8|next-nex | event - compiled client and server successfully in 1800 ms (189 modules)

8|next-next18  | warn  - using beta Middleware (not covered by semver) - https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/beta-middleware
8|next-next18  | wait  - compiling / (client and server)...
8|next-next18  | wait  - compiling...
8|next-next18  | event - compiled client and server successfully in 887 ms (413 modules)
8|next-next18  | error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')
8|next-next18  | wait  - compiling /_error (client and server)...
8|next-next18  | wait  - compiling...
8|next-next18  | event - compiled client and server successfully in 408 ms (414 modules)
```

Clicking page reload in the browser 5 times results in a repeat of the error which I think stems from a failure of 
```
8|next-next18  | error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')
8|next-next18  | error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')
8|next-next18  | error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')
8|next-next18  | error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')
8|next-next18  | error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')
```
If I disable experimental concurrent features in next.config.js msw works fine again.

### Expected Behavior

To have msw function correctly with experimental concurrent features enabled.

msw will be a great testing tool to evaluate React18 suspense data fetching.

### To Reproduce

- install https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-msw
- confirm index.ts loads correctly with React 17.
- updgrade to React 18 with npm install next@latest react@rc react-dom@rc or yarn add next@latest react@rc react-dom@rc
- confirm index.ts loads correctly with React 18.
- configure next.config.js to include

```
module.exports = {
    experimental: {
      concurrentFeatures: true,
    },
}
```

- observe error on server console: "error - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')"
- reload the page a few times and not reocurrence of error.
author: laurencefass

title: next/script does not trigger onLoad callback when used with beforeInteractive strategy
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.2

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.15

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Linux

### How are you deploying your application?

next dev

### Describe the Bug

According to the [docs](https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/script#executing-code-after-loading-onload), `onLoad` callback of `next/script` component can be used either with `beforeInteractive` and `afterInteractive` strategies. But when using with `beforeInteractive` strategy, `onLoad` callback never gets called.

### Expected Behavior

Expect `onLoad` callback to be called when loading script with `beforeInteractive` strategy

### To Reproduce

Using the same example of the docs, with stripe script: hasLoaded is never set to true and stripe has loaded never gets printed.
I get the same behavior in the latest version of next.

Here is a minimal reproducible repo: https://github.com/loft-br/before-interactive-on-load-bug
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/76054805/148971151-303362fe-092f-45f8-ba43-5f0ae7fb999a.png)

title: Stylesheet not working in a component under Head tag
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

sh: 1: pnpm: not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #52-Ubuntu SMP Thu Sep 10 10:58:49 UTC 2020
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.12.0
      npm: 8.1.0
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8-canary.20
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8-canary.20

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.12.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome/codesandbox

### What operating system are you using?

Ubuntu

### How are you deploying your application?

codesandbox

### Describe the Bug

When I put Stylesheet inside <HEAD> tag it works fine but If I move Stylesheet to a separate component (called HeadMeta) stylesheet not loaded and completly missing from DOM tree.

Check the Google `Roboto` font in the source!

This works:
```js
<Head>
        <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" />
        <meta name="description" content={siteDescription} />
        <meta
          property="og:image"
          content={`https://og-image.vercel.app/${encodeURI(
            siteTitle
          )}.png?theme=light&md=0&fontSize=75px&images=https%3A%2F%2Fassets.vercel.com%2Fimage%2Fupload%2Ffront%2Fassets%2Fdesign%2Fnextjs-black-logo.svg`}
        />
        <meta name="og:title" content={siteTitle} />
        <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
        <link
          rel="stylesheet"
          href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Roboto:300,400,500,700&display=swap"
        />

        <link
          rel="stylesheet"
          href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons"
        />
        <meta name="viewport" content="initial-scale=1, width=device-width" />
      </Head>
```

this isn't:

```js
<Head>
        <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" />
        <meta name="description" content={siteDescription} />
        <meta
          property="og:image"
          content={`https://og-image.vercel.app/${encodeURI(
            siteTitle
          )}.png?theme=light&md=0&fontSize=75px&images=https%3A%2F%2Fassets.vercel.com%2Fimage%2Fupload%2Ffront%2Fassets%2Fdesign%2Fnextjs-black-logo.svg`}
        />
        <meta name="og:title" content={siteTitle} />
        <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
        <HeadMeta />

        <link
          rel="stylesheet"
          href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons"
        />
        <meta name="viewport" content="initial-scale=1, width=device-width" />
      </Head>
```

HeadMeta.js
```js
import * as React from "react";

function HeadMeta() {
  return (
    <React.Fragment>
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Roboto:300,400,500,700&display=swap"
      />
    </React.Fragment>
  );
}

export default HeadMeta;

```


### Expected Behavior

Roboto font loads when stylesheet is in separate component.

### To Reproduce

Move stylesheet into separate component inside `<Head>` component.


```js
<Head>
        <HeadMeta />
</Head>
```

HeadMeta.js
```js
import * as React from "react";

function HeadMeta() {
  return (
    <React.Fragment>
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Roboto:300,400,500,700&display=swap"
      />
    </React.Fragment>
  );
}

export default HeadMeta;

```
author: kepes

title: Add support for middleware rewrite proxy headers
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

I would like to be able to specify headers to be sent by the proxy that is running behind the `NextResponse.rewrite` middleware response.

I understand it's possible to set headers in the `NextResponse.rewrite` response, but these headers are sent to the user visiting the next.js page, what I would like is to set headers that the destination server will receive.

Upon digging into the next.js internals, I understand we'd need to provide such headers in the `Proxy` instance within `proxyRequest` inside `/packages/next/server/base-server.ts`

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like to be able to, perhaps, provide a config object as second parameter of `NextResponse.rewrite` which could accept something like `forwardHeaders` that accepts a Headers object and this would then be merged with the headers that the proxy may already need to send.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently, my alternative solution is preform this rewrite using nginx and provide the headers there. If these headers can be forwarded within nextjs, I believe this feature will be even more powerful.
author: johnsardine

title: nextjs 11 adding no cache headers to document
body: ### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) _No response_ ### What version of Next.js are you using? 11.1.3 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 16.13.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? We use a custom express server. We use NODE_ENV=production next build ./src followed by pm2 start ### Describe the Bug nextjs 10 never used to add cache control headers to the document when using a custom express server, but with nextjs 11 the server now sends cache control headers (private, no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate) for the document. If the pages are behind a CDN, the new headers forces the CDN to fetch the fresh version on every request effectively making the CDN ineffective. ### Expected Behavior If you compare it to version 10 it should not have sent any cache control headers ### To Reproduce Create a nextjs application with custom express server using nextjs 11. Create a route and attach a page to the route using next.render. Do a production build (not a development built) and open the custom route and inspect the response headers. Attaching screenshots for reference.

title: next/image doesn't use custom device sizes when used inside component library
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

/bin/sh: yarn: command not found
/bin/sh: pnpm: command not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.10.0
      npm: 7.24.0
      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8-canary.22
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8-canary.22

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.10.0

### What browser are you using?

Edge

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next run dev

### Describe the Bug

The `Image` component doesn't use the custom `deviceSizes` specified in next.config.js, but uses the default `deviceSizes` instead. 

The bug can be reproduced using the following scenario:

1. An image must be loaded with the `priority` flag (it only happens during SSR)
2. The next/image `Image` component must be used inside a component library. I've tried copying the wrapper component to the app, which causes the error to disappear

The image is not loaded and the console produces the following warning: 

```
Warning: Prop `srcSet` did not match. Server: "/_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=640&q=75 640w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=750&q=75 750w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=828&q=75 828w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=1080&q=75 1080w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=1200&q=75 1200w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=1920&q=75 1920w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=2048&q=75 2048w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=3840&q=75 3840w" Client: "/_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=123&q=75 123w, /_next/image?url=%2Fvercel.svg&w=456&q=75 456w"
```

Upon further investigation, I've found that `process.env.__NEXT_IMAGE_OPTS` is `undefined` when using the `Image` component in the component library, which causes Next to use the default values instead. There's also a [similar issue](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/30494) regarding the `Link` component.


### Expected Behavior

The `Image` component uses the custom device sizes and loads the image correctly.

### To Reproduce

I've created an example repository to reproduce the issue: https://github.com/johnnyomair/next-image-bug-demo

1. Clone the repository
2. `cd app/`
3. `npm run dev`
4. Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000)

The image inside the first card should not be loaded, and an error should be shown instead.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48853629/149184527-f5e8da83-e020-435c-9dba-41b4e9636376.png)
author: johnnyomair

body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```bash
Operating System:
  Platform: darwin

  Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64
Binaries:
  Node: 16.13.1
  npm: 8.1.2
  Yarn: 1.22.17
  pnpm: N/A
Relevant packages:
  next: 12.0.8
  react: 17.0.2
  react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS 12.1 Monterey

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

While navigating trough all links in sidebar, hitting the back-button in browser "freezes" loading of previous pages when we get to a specific page.  This is only the case when using `next/link` and navigating to dynamic routes.

Hitting back again does nothing and stays "frozen" until the history reaches a page that is not dynamically routed or was not navigated to trough next/link. Meanwhile hitting forwards in browser works correctly and "unfreezes" the navigation. 

##

Notes from testing issue:
- Path that ends up "freezing" is correctly set in `getStaticPaths`.
- No data is loaded in debugger->network when clicking browser back-button to that specific link. Example in recording 👇 Event listeners or 
```jsx
useEffect(() => {
    demoRouter.beforePopState(({ url, as }) => {
      console.log(JSON.stringify({ url, as }, null, 2));
      return true;
    });
  }, []);
```
in `_app.tsx` does not run when this happens either.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26967723/149218296-7092bc9e-8d28-4ec0-9efa-937167e4dbc3.mov

In recording, navigation freezes when it should have navigated to "Accordion", as seen when hitting back twice. One can see the URL and Head <title> update correctly at the 10 second mark while still rendering "Alert".

Next example is logging `beforePopState` in `_app.tsx` with this snippet 
```jsx
useEffect(() => {
    demoRouter.beforePopState(({ url, as }) => {
      console.log(JSON.stringify({ url, as }, null, 2));
      return true;
    });
  }, []);
```


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26967723/149219439-df3fee65-b94a-4548-bbaa-777ade4f7b7a.mov

At the 12 second mark one can see the "beforePopState" run while hitting browser forwards-button to "Accordion" after it not  logging while hitting the back-button. The URL differs from the expected output here, while `as` is as expected. 
```json
{
  "url": "/designsystem/[...slug]?slug=side&slug=alert",
  "as": "/designsystem/side/accordion"
}
```


## 

Usage of next/link 
- ScLink is here a styled-components `<a>`-tag, using native `<a>` does not fix the issue
- onClick does not preventDefault
- Using shallow or prefetch=false does not make a difference
```jsx
<NextLink href={`/${item.link.slug.current}`} passHref>
	  <ScLink
	    onClick={(e) => {
	      onClick && onClick();
	      logNavigation(e);
	    }}
	    active={
	      pageProps?.page
	        ? pageProps.page.slug === item.link.slug.current
	        : false
	    }
	  >
		    {item.title}
	  </ScLink>
</NextLink>
```

[Failing link-code in sidebar code using next/link](https://github.com/navikt/verktoykassen/blob/issue/nextjs-routing/website/components/layout/menu/DesignsystemMenu.tsx)

[_app.tsx](https://github.com/navikt/verktoykassen/blob/issue/nextjs-routing/website/pages/_app.tsx)

[Dynamic routing ([...slug].tsx)](https://github.com/navikt/verktoykassen/blob/issue/nextjs-routing/website/pages/designsystem/%5B...slug%5D.tsx)

### Expected Behavior

Hitting backwards in browser always loads the previous page when having used `next/link` to navigate to/from that specific dynamically routed page.

### To Reproduce

Bug in production can be tested [here](https://design.nav.no/designsystem/side/komponenter) 

Clone [branch in repo](https://github.com/navikt/verktoykassen/tree/issue/nextjs-routing)
```batch
// While in repo root
yarn install
yarn build:next
yarn serve:next
```
- open http://localhost:3000/designsystem/side/komponenter
- In sidebar, navigate from "Oversikt" -> "Accordion" -> "Alert"
- Hit back-button in browser

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26967723/149222058-8daab94d-a4e3-467d-819e-3dfc40a35a2b.mov

title: Optional catch all routes not working as expected with Internationalized Routing and getStaticParams with path: [] and fallback: 'blocking'
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) Operating System: Platform: win32 Arch: x64 Version: Windows 10 Pro Binaries: Node: 16.13.0 npm: 8.1.2 Yarn: N/A pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.8 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.8 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 16.13.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? Windows ### How are you deploying your application? Vercel ### Describe the Bug **Optional catch all routes** doesn't work as expected when it is used under the following conditions: - Nested inside a dynamic route. - Using getStaticPaths with `paths: []` and `fallback: 'blocking'`. - Using Internationalized Routing. - When it's deployed (e.g. Vercel), locally runs as properly). In this scenario "**optional catch all routes**" works as "**catch all routes**". ### Expected Behavior so for example this file: _/articles/[category]/[[...pages]].tsx_ should catch, among others, the following routes: _/articles/sport_ _/articles/sport/page/1_ but _/articles/sport_ (that would be the special route that **optional catch all routes** has as a target), results in 404. _/articles/sport/page/1_ loads fine. ### To Reproduce Set up internationalized Routes on next.config.ts ```javascript module.exports = { reactStrictMode: true, i18n: { locales: ['en', 'es'], defaultLocale: 'en', }, } ``` Create a simple file with a parent dynamic route and an optional catch all route: _/pages/articles/[category]/[...pages]].tsx_ ```typescript interface PageProps { locale: string params: ParsedUrlQuery | undefined } const Page = ({ locale, params }: PageProps) => { return (

It Loads

  • Locale: {locale}
    • {params &&
  • Params: 
    {JSON.stringify(params, null, 4)}
    • }
) } Page.getLayout = (page: ReactElement) => ( {page} ) export default Page export const getStaticPaths = async () => { return { paths: [], fallback: 'blocking', } } export const getStaticProps = async ({ locale, params }: GetStaticPropsContext) => { return { props: { locale, params, }, revalidate: 60 } } ``` Deploy. GitHub repository: [here](https://github.com/ramirezsandin/optional-catch-all-routes) Test site in Vercel: [live site](https://optional-catch-all-routes.vercel.app/)

title: Infinite render loop with server components when running next dev
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

**Operating System:**
Platform: win32
Arch: x64
Version: Windows 10 Pro

**Binaries:**
Node: 16.13.0
npm: 8.1.0

**Relevant packages:**
next: 12.0.8
react: 18.0.0-rc.0
react-dom: 18.0.0-rc.0

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8 (issue appears in 12.0.8-canary.13)

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.0

### What browser are you using?

not relevant

### What operating system are you using?

Windows

### How are you deploying your application?

next dev

### Describe the Bug

Using `next dev`, a page rendering as a server component seems to render in an infinite loop. This can be observed in developer tools Network tab, where a `test?__flight__=1` can be seen repeat itself infinitely.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10454463/149292707-593aacbd-7bc8-4895-ae21-27f1ad5f54fb.png)

This problem _does not_ occur when running `next build` and `next start`. It is only observed when running `next dev`.

The problem starts occurring in [12.0.8-canary.13](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v12.0.8-canary.13).

Any code in the component root gets repeated as the component rerenders on server, so I believe this problem would cause any data fetching done in the server component to repeat infinitely.

### Expected Behavior

The development mode works similarly to production build, without the infinite calls and rerenders.

### To Reproduce

1. Clone the [example repository](https://github.com/marozzocom/next-rsc-dev-issue)
2. run `npm install`
3. run `npm run dev`
4. navigate to `http://localhost:3000/test`
5. open developer tools and observe the Network tab

**Edits made in example repository after create-next-app**

- enable experimental features in `next.config.js`
- add customized `pages/_document.js` page
- add `pages/test.server.js` page
