title: Build fails when `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` TS config option is enabled
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #1 SMP PREEMPT Wed, 22 Dec 2021 09:23:54 +0000
    Binaries:
      Node: 17.3.0
      npm: N/A
      Yarn: 3.1.1
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

17.3.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome (but irrelevant)

### What operating system are you using?

Arch Linux

### How are you deploying your application?

next build

### Describe the Bug

When the TypeScript `compilerOptions` option `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` is enabled, `next build` fails with the following error:
```
Failed to compile.

../../node_modules/next/dist/server/dev/next-dev-server.d.ts:63:5
Type error: Property 'generateRoutes' in type 'DevServer' is not assignable to the same property in base type 'NextNodeServer'.
```

My understanding is that `DevServer` has an "explicitly" optional type containing `{generateRoutes: Route | undefined}` while `NextNodeServer` has an "implicit" optional type containing `{generateRoutes?: Route}`. `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` disallows explicitly assigning `undefined` to optional types.

### Expected Behavior

I'm unsure if I _expect_ this to pass. But it would probably be good to be compatible with this option.

This bug report might be more of an improvement suggestion.

### To Reproduce

1. Set up a Typescript Next.js project normally (i.e. via the example or otherwise).
2. Modify `tsconfig.json` to have the following (along with what was there to begin with):
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": false,
  }
}
```
3. Run `next build` (`next dev` works fine), and watch it fail on the "Checking validity of types" step.

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v17.1.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

MacOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next run dev

### Describe the Bug

The following code executes differently in my browser than in next.js:

```
const myFunction = () => {
  for (let j = 0; j <= 3; j++) {
    for (const _ of []) {
    }

    if (j === 1) {
      console.log("before set timeout, j is:", j)

      setTimeout(() => {
        console.log("in timeout: j is", j)
      }, 50)
    }
  }

  return null
}

myFunction()
```

In my browser, this code correctly prints:

```
before set timeout, j is: 1
in timeout: j is 1
```

When I paste this into the top level of index.tsx, run `yarn run dev` and navigate my browser to localhost:3000, I see:

```
before set timeout, j is: 1
in timeout: j is 4
```

### Expected Behavior

I expected the output to be the same whether in browser console or in next.js.

### To Reproduce

1. Create a new next app with `npx create-next-app my-app`
2. Paste the code into pages/index.js
3. `yarn run dev` and look at the console

### Describe the Bug

We have found a bug when using Next 12 with [Gasket](https://gasket.dev). When the custom `_app` uses `getInitialProps`, the process hangs and never finishes. 

This is reproducable with a very simple custom build script, such as in [this repo](https://github.com/kinetifex/next-12-custom-build-bug).

When calling the Next `build` function from a script, the process hangs and never exits.

### Expected Behavior

Running a build by executing the `next/dist/build` function from a script completes and exits successfully.

### To Reproduce

Clone [this repo](https://github.com/kinetifex/next-12-custom-build-bug), and run the custom build script.

```
git clone git@github.com:kinetifex/next-12-custom-build-bug.git
cd next-12-custom-build-bug
yarn
yarn custom-build
```

title: Disabling `esmExternals` generates a misleading warning message
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: win32
      Arch: x64
      Version: Windows 10 Home
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.2
      npm: 8.3.2
      Yarn: 1.22.10
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:        
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2  

### What version of Next.js are you using?

v12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.6

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Windows

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

Since Next.js `11.1.3-canary.69`, ESM externals has been turned on by default using the `experimental: { esmExternals: true }` in this [commit](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/29878).

Unfortunately ESM external is still not compatible with a lot of packages and the only way to turn it off is by setting the following config:

`experimental: { esmExternals: false }`

The problem is that any configuration set under `experimental` triggers the following warning logs, which for this case seems misleading:

```sh
warn  - You have enabled experimental feature(s).
warn  - Experimental features are not covered by semver, and may cause unexpected or broken application behavior. Use them at your own risk.
```

This is related to https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/33148

### Expected Behavior

I would expect that `esmExternals` is moved from `experimental` to "top-level" and stops triggering the warning.

### To Reproduce

Simply set `experimental: { esmExternals: false }` in `next.config.js`

title: @next/mdx ignores configuration function settings in next.config.js
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #202111250933~1638201579~21.04~09f1aa7-Ubuntu SMP Tue Nov 30 02:
    Binaries:
      Node: 15.4.0
      npm: 7.0.15
      Yarn: 1.22.10
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

next 12.0.8 + @next/mdx 12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v15.4.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Pop!_OS (Ubuntu-based Linux distribution)

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

**I'm opening this issue so I can reference it in a PR that I'm hoping to submit tomorrow. I have a fix working locally.**

When I pass a configuration function into `@next/mdx`'s `withMDX()` function in `next.config.js`, `withMDX()` ignores the settings defined in the function . 

### Expected Behavior

Because the [docs](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/introduction) for `next.config.js` show functions as a valid way to define Next's configuration, I would expect that I could pass a configuration function into `withMDX()` and it would be able to merge the executed function output with its own settings.

### To Reproduce

Create a new Next project.

Install `@next/mdx`.

Replace the contents of `next.config.js` with the following:

```js
const withMDX = require("@next/mdx")();

const nextConfig = (phase, { defaultConfig }) => {
  return {
    reactStrictMode: true,
    pageExtensions: ["ts", "tsx", "js", "jsx", "mdx"],
  };
};

module.exports = withMDX(nextConfig);
```

Create `pages/hello-world.mdx` with the follow contents:

```mdx
# Hello, World
```

Run `npm run dev`

Navigate to `http://localhost:3000/hello-world` and see a 404 error page.

title: Redirect with `basePath: false` and i18n does not work
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #29~20.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Fri Jan 14 00:32:30 UTC 2022
    Binaries:
      Node: 17.2.0
      npm: 8.3.0
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: 6.28.0
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.9-canary.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

17.2.0

### What browser are you using?

Firefox, Chromium

### What operating system are you using?

Linux Mint

### How are you deploying your application?

next dev

### Describe the Bug

When using i18n and a basePath in the next config, redirects with `basePath` set to false do not work for any paths that are not in the list of i18n locales.

If the `i18n` block in the example below is removed, the redirects work as expected.

Redirects from source urls that happen to be part of the locales do work for some reason (ex: /en /fr /de).

### Expected Behavior

Given the config below, a request coming in at `/` should be redirected to `/example`. Given that the `locale` property on the redirect is set to false, I do not expect the routing to be affected by a locale prefix.

### To Reproduce

Create a new app using: `npx create-next-app@latest`

Update `next.config.js` to the following:
```js
const basePath = '/example'

module.exports = {
  basePath,

  i18n: {
    locales: ['en', 'fr', 'de'],
    defaultLocale: 'en',
  },

  async redirects() {
    return [
      // Doesn't work (404, no redirect)
      { source: '/', destination: basePath, basePath: false, permanent: false, locale: false },
      { source: '/something', destination: basePath, basePath: false, permanent: false, locale: false },
      { source: '/nl', destination: basePath, basePath: false, permanent: false, locale: false },

      // Does work (redirect)
      { source: '/en', destination: basePath, basePath: false, permanent: false, locale: false },
    ]
  },
}
```

Start using `npm run dev`

Open the page in a browser, note that it does not redirect from `/`, `/something`, or `/nl`.

title: Internationalized routes don't work with dynamic routes without getStaticPaths
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: x64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Wed Nov 10 22:23:07 PST 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.14~1/RELEASE_X86_64 Binaries: Node: 16.13.0 npm: 8.1.0 Yarn: 1.22.10 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.8 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.8 (latest) ### What version of Node.js are you using? 16.13.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? next start ### Describe the Bug dynamic routes with i18n routing fall into an infinite redirect loop. ### Expected Behavior When creating a dynamic route I should be able to access the link with no infinite redirect loop. ### To Reproduce in a basic next.js app, add the dummy example from the docs as to build a dynamic page: 1. pages/post/[pid].tsx 2. Then make this the content of the page, as per the docs: ```js import { useRouter } from 'next/router' const Post = () => { const router = useRouter() const { pid } = router.query return

Post: {pid}

} export default Post ``` 3. Now create a next.config.js file to introduce i18n internationalized routes per domain: ```js module.exports = { basePath: "/app", i18n: { localeDetection: false, locales: [ "de-DE", "de-AT", "en-US", ], defaultLocale: "en-US", domains: [ { domain: 'localhost:3000', locales: [ "de-DE", "de-AT", "en-US", ], defaultLocale: process.env.APP_LOCALE || "en-US", http: true, }, ], }, }; ``` 4. Now run yarn build && yarn start to run the app 5. Navigate to localhost:3000/app/post/1 You should be able to see printed in the screen: Post 1, but instead, you see `Post` and the page keeps refreshing. I prepared this simple example based on the docs: https://github.com/sergioviniciuss/next-dynamic-routing-i18n

Render the screen after a refresh the browser without this error.

title: Disable Irrelevant Warnings and Output
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

```
API response for /file exceeds 4MB. This will cause the request to fail in a future version. 
https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/api-routes-body-size-limit
```

- I am not utilizing serverless functions.
- I do not plan on utilizing serverless functions.
- I do not plan on utilizing my project for more than a dozen users.
- I am utilizing an external resolver for said API route.

Thus, this response size limit does not apply to me.

Utilizing a CDN is overkill for my project. I do not want to serve my files with a CDN for such an application, especially since it incurs cost.

This warning is very helpful, but it is in no way applicable to my project or how I plan to use it. It's uselessly cluttering up my output.

Can I turn this warning off? Also, will Next.js eventually break my API __even when its run in an environment__ where the 4 MB size limit does not apply? That would be **very** annoying.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Additional configuration could be added to `next.config.js`. 

Any non-error messages should reference an `outputConfig` entry and allow some outputs to be turned off.

```ts
import getConfig from 'next/config';

if (getConfig().outputConfig['api-routes-body-size-limit'] != false)
    console.log('API response for /file exceeds 4MB...');
```

`next.config.mjs`
```ts
// Used to toggle various behaviors for Next.js's output.
export const outputConfig = {
    // false = "silence" / dont show
    "api-routes-body-size-limit": false,
    // names should correspond to links shown in the output so its easy to keep track of them.
}
```

Again, this warning is not applicable to me and I just want to be able to turn it off.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

- Scroll more than I need to because my output is obscured by multiple instances of said warning.
- Reinvent the wheel and write an encapsulating program to filter it out of the output (not worth the effort)

title: Ensure rewrites, i18n and middleware are working properly with the web server

author: shuding

title: Cannot A/B test static pages using middleware
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Edge (Chromium)

### What operating system are you using?

Windows 10

### How are you deploying your application?

Azure App Service

### Describe the Bug

Relevant information specifically for this bug: We host Next.js in Azure on our own in Azure.

We tried to run an A/B experiment using the new middleware functionality on two **static** pages: index.tsx and index2.tsx.

The problem we are having is that for static pages Next.js emits a cache control header that makes our CDN cache one page or another. And we cannot override cache control headers (to set it to no-cache) for static pages in next.config.js. Here's what the [docs](https://nextjs.org/docs/going-to-production#caching) say: Cache-Control headers set in next.config.js will be overwritten in production to ensure that static assets can be cached effectively.

### Expected Behavior

- Do not emit cache control headers that cache a page when it is served through a rewrite from middleware.
- OR: Allow overriding Cache-Control header for static pages in next.config.js, at least if middleware is involved in rewriting them.

### To Reproduce

Create two static pages, have a _middleware randomly choose between them. Notice if you put a CDN in front of the website that A/B testing will fail to work as expected because one of the sides (A or B) will get cached by the CDN.

### Describe the Bug

In this page: [Disabling Automatic Locale Detection](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/i18n-routing#disabling-automatic-locale-detection) it says that you can disable the locale detection but it still will provide the locale info (this is more disabling routing than locale, I think).  
The example shows that you have only to set ``localeDetection: false`` in next.config.js

This is the error:
> Error: Specified i18n.locales should be an Array received undefined.
See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/invalid-i18n-config

### Expected Behavior

Locale is detected and available by _userRouter_ and _getStaticProps_ _context_.

### To Reproduce

Createa  basic project. 
Change next.config.js to be like the example:
```javascript
module.exports = {
  i18n: {
    localeDetection: false,
  },
}
```

Just rin the application within ``yarn dev`` (it calls a script with: ``yarn install && next dev``)

title: Generate functions-manifest.json

body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) Operating System: Platform: linux Arch: x64 Version: #202111250933~1638201579~21.04~09f1aa7-Ubuntu SMP Tue Nov 30 02: Binaries: Node: 15.4.0 npm: 7.0.15 Yarn: 1.22.10 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.8 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.8 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 15.4.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? Pop!_OS (Ubuntu-based Linux distribution) ### How are you deploying your application? next start ### Describe the Bug The `next build` command is able to process negative-lookbehind regular expressions in the `pageExtensions` setting correctly, while `next dev` does not. The following page extension should match `.js` files, while ignoring files with a `.ignore.js` extension. ```js // next.config.js module.exports = { pageExtensions: ["(? ({ pageExtensions: phase === PHASE_DEVELOPMENT_SERVER ? ["ignore.js", "(?I am a test page.; } ``` Run `npm run dev` then navigate to `http://localhost:3000/page`. You'll see a 404 error instead of the page. Now run `npm run build && npm run start` then navigate to `http://localhost:3000/page`. You'll see the page rendering correctly. --- If you like, you can also confirm that ignored pages are not accessible. Create `pages/hidden.ignore.js` with the content below then visit `http://localhost:3000/hidden` using both `next dev` and `next start`. It will return a 404 on both methods (this is currently working correctly). ```js export default function HiddenPage(props) { return
I am a hidden page.
; } ```

Vercel ### Describe the Bug On all previous versions of next/styled-jsx, my site has built just fine. Upgrading to `12.0.8`, the upgrade of `styled-jsx` from `5.0.0-beta.3` to `5.0.0-beta.6` broke the project because of the selector `input::-webkit-slider-thumb:active`. (Source: https://toughengineer.github.io/demo/slider-styler/slider-styler.html) You can see it live in action on the sliders on https://watershedclimate.com/wfh-calculator ### To Reproduce I've created a minimal reproduction project here: https://codesandbox.io/s/wild-river-vlyf2?file=/components/input_range.js Commenting out this selector, as well as the accompanying `::-moz-range-thumb:active`, allows the site to build.

title: Script component results in different attributes based on the strategy
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

next info output:
```
Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Wed Nov 10 22:23:07 PST 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.14~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.0
      npm: 8.1.0
      Yarn: 1.22.11
      pnpm: 6.24.2
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

I premise that I am not sure it is a problem, however I have not found this behavior documented anywhere.

I'm using the Script component and I'm seeing some differences when setting the strategy to `beforeInteractive` compared to other strategies.
The main difference I'm seeing is on the `defer` and `crossorigin` attributes. I set `corssorigin=anonymous` and `defer=false` in some of our scripts.
Setting `beforeInteractive` strategy the ending DOM element always has the defer attribute (no matter what I pass to the component) and the crossorigin is ignored.

Examples:
with code like this
```js
<Script
  src="test.js"
  id="test"
  crossOrigin="anonymous"
  data-test="test"
  defer={false}
  strategy="beforeInteractive"
/>
```
the output is
```html
<script src="test.js" id="test" data-test="test" defer="" data-nscript="beforeInteractive"></script>
```
with code like this
```js
<Script
  src="test.js"
  id="test"
  crossOrigin="anonymous"
  data-test="test"
  defer={false}
  strategy="afterInteractive"
/>
```
the output is
```html
<script src="test.js" id="test" crossorigin="anonymous" data-test="test" defer="false" data-nscript="afterInteractive"></script>
```

### Expected Behavior

`defer` and `crossorigin` attributes are not changed based on strategy

### To Reproduce

I created [a repository](https://github.com/albertodeago/next-script-bug-repro) to reproduce it easily.
Just clone it, `npm run dev`, and open localhost:3000

In the console two scripts are being printed, the two have the same attributes except for the strategy.

title: Url imports resources with Cache-Control: no-cache fails
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.11.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next dev / next start

### Describe the Bug

According to https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/url-imports , setting `Cache-Control: no-cache` on the response should force next to fetch the url each time if I understand correctly.

When I try to access a page in this conditions, the compilation stucks and sometimes this message appears:
```
error - Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open 'app/next.lock/.lock.3955.json'
[Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open 'app/next.lock/.lock.3955.json'] {
  errno: -2,
  code: 'ENOENT',
  syscall: 'open',
  path: 'app/next.lock/.lock.3955.json'
}
```

Moreover, I cannot run "next build" because no next.lock exists.

### Expected Behavior

In dev mode, the url imported module should be fetch each time to build the page.

When building for production, it should fetch the url imported module in order to statically render the page.

When using ISR, and during `next start`, it should re fetch the url imported module in order to rebuild a specific page. No .next.lock should be version controlled then as url imported modules should be fetched each time. Maybe `next build` should always fetch missing url imported module because as of now, it has no ways to know if that resource is served with no-cache directive or not

### To Reproduce

Clone https://github.com/SBNTT/nextjs_url_imports_issue

```
cd modules/module_a
yarn install
yarn build
yarn start
```
http://localhost:3001/index.js should be accessible with `Cache-Control: no-cache` header in its response

Now, cd inside app directory and start it in development mode. Accessing http://localhost:3000 will fail with the internal server error mentioned above
author: SBNTT

title: Allow jsconfig-path-plugin to supports specified packages in node_modules
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Currently, my team is using [next-transpile-modules](https://github.com/martpie/next-transpile-modules) to "inject" a package (our UI library) into next.js application. We're using [linaria](https://github.com/callstack/linaria) as styled engine and to be able to do theming, we're using [babel-plugin-module-resolver](https://github.com/tleunen/babel-plugin-module-resolver) to "point to" the correct theme that we want. This approach works.

However, if the built-in `jsconfig-path-plugin` can do the similar task, then we don't need to use the `babel-plugin-module-resolver` anymore. 



### Describe the solution you'd like

Instead of using fixed [NODE_MODULES_REGEX](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/build/webpack/plugins/jsconfig-paths-plugin.ts#L202), can you allow us passing in an option object to control this condition.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Or if you have a better way to achieve it, please tell me. Thank you so much.

title: SVG error generating .next/static/media folder v11.1.3 vs v12
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

v12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v16.13.2

### What browser are you using?

Chrome, IE, Edge

### What operating system are you using?

Windows 10

### How are you deploying your application?

localhost, 'yarn && yarn dev'

### Describe the Bug

Hi, I'm considerably new to this so maybe I'll need some help to provide all necessary info, but I'll try my best.
I joined a new project which I can't share code, but it was working perfectly for other teammembers on Linux / Mac. I think it might be related to Windows as I tried and the error still happens in different browsers as Edge, Internet Explorer and Chrome, and until now I'm the only windows user I'm aware of in the project.

When I use v12 or above, my 'yarn && yarn dev' returns success for compilation, but once I open the browser it just loads forever. Whenever I refresh the page it complains about not being able to find '\.next\static\media\svg+xml,<.ecc90d41''.
So that's not an config error otherwise other teammembers should have the same issue, I could try and create such file, but windows don't like < character on filenames, this can't be the root cause, or can it?
But, back to the browser. If I refresh the webpage then it prints 'Internal Server Error' on the screen and media folder gets created, but with no files inside (I can't stop thinking about < character now...)

So I rolled back to v11.0.0 and it works. Went upwards as much as I could in v11 and ended up on v11.1.3 which also works, so there is some change in between v11.1.3 and v12 that created all of this for me, but not for all other teammembers. 

In v11.1.3 the project runs locally and still there isn't an media folder inside .next/static/ therefore there is no file with < character in the name that I can find, and there is a webpack folder now, but nothing inside resembles an svg or the missing media folder, so I think it's because it doesn't uses webpack5 yet?

I've tried the same node version as some of the teammembers where the project works as expected, but it still returns the same error for me.

### Expected Behavior

type yarn then yarn dev at the console and runs the project locally.
Expected to have a folder called media with a file named svg+xml,<.ecc90d41 inside it before running the project, but the folder is only created when I refresh the webpage, as initially it just keep loading forever, but the folder is then created empty
![Screen Shot 2022-01-26 at 20 05 13](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43976069/151179333-fcbea8db-490d-49a1-b6a7-747f46e8cb33.png)
.

### To Reproduce

Only me who is a windows user at the project get this error, I received a screenshot with the same filename from a linux user, then I tried to manually create it even tho .next would be recreated at every script I run, but < is no valid filename character in Windows, I tried to create the file both on VSCode and in File Explorer, which I seriously believe to be the issue now after describing the problem.. I can run the project in next@v11.1.3 without any issue and this version doesn't create a media folder inside .next/static, therefore there is no filename using < as a character
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43976069/151178929-d80d320b-7a3d-487f-a5af-61a68ff60936.png)
.

body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin

      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Tue Oct 12 18:33:42 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.8~1/RELEASE_X86_64

      Node: 16.11.0

      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.11.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Docker on Azure

### Describe the Bug

We are currently using the automatic copying of traced files from the output standalone (https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/output-file-tracing#automatically-copying-traced-files-experimental).
This is working great so far.
We are using the generated `server.js` to serve our application.
However, we noticed our resources were not being compressed automatically anymore, which was the case when we were still using `next start` before.
When I have a look in the `server.js` file which is generated, I noticed that the `compress` configuration option was `false` by default.
Is there a way to overwrite this?
Wouldn't it be more logical to put `compress` on `true` by default?

Server.js contents:
```javascript
process.env.NODE_ENV = 'production'
process.chdir(__dirname)
const NextServer = require('next/dist/server/next-server').default
const http = require('http')
const path = require('path')

let handler

const server = http.createServer(async (req, res) => {
  try {
    await handler(req, res)
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err)
    res.statusCode = 500
    res.end('internal server error')
  }
})
const currentPort = parseInt(process.env.PORT, 10) || 3000

server.listen(currentPort, err => {
  if (err) {
    console.error('Failed to start server', err)
    process.exit(1)
  }
  const addr = server.address()
  const nextServer = new NextServer({
    hostname: 'localhost',
    port: currentPort,
    dir: path.join(__dirname),
    dev: false,
    conf: {
      env: {},
      webpack: null,
      webpackDevMiddleware: null,
      eslint: {ignoreDuringBuilds: false},
      typescript: {ignoreBuildErrors: false, tsconfigPath: 'tsconfig.json'},
      distDir: './.next',
      cleanDistDir: true,
      assetPrefix: '',
      configOrigin: 'next.config.js',
      useFileSystemPublicRoutes: true,
      generateEtags: true,
      pageExtensions: ['tsx', 'ts', 'jsx', 'js'],
      target: 'server',
      poweredByHeader: true,
      compress: true,
      analyticsId: '',
      images: {
        deviceSizes: [640, 750, 828, 1080, 1200, 1920, 2048, 3840],
        imageSizes: [16, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256, 384],
        path: '/_next/image',
        loader: 'default',
        domains: [
          'assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com',
          'preview-assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com',
        ],
        disableStaticImages: false,
        minimumCacheTTL: 60,
        formats: ['image/webp'],
      },
      devIndicators: {
        buildActivity: true,
        buildActivityPosition: 'bottom-right',
      },
      onDemandEntries: {maxInactiveAge: 15000, pagesBufferLength: 2},
      amp: {canonicalBase: ''},
      basePath: '',
      sassOptions: {},
      trailingSlash: false,
      i18n: {defaultLocale: 'en', locales: ['en', 'nl']},
      productionBrowserSourceMaps: false,
      optimizeFonts: true,
      excludeDefaultMomentLocales: true,
      serverRuntimeConfig: {},
      publicRuntimeConfig: {},
      reactStrictMode: false,
      httpAgentOptions: {keepAlive: true},
      outputFileTracing: true,
      staticPageGenerationTimeout: 60,
      swcMinify: false,
      experimental: {
        cpus: 11,
        sharedPool: true,
        plugins: false,
        profiling: false,
        isrFlushToDisk: true,
        workerThreads: false,
        pageEnv: false,
        optimizeImages: false,
        optimizeCss: false,
        scrollRestoration: false,
        externalDir: false,
        reactRoot: false,
        disableOptimizedLoading: false,
        gzipSize: true,
        swcFileReading: true,
        craCompat: false,
        esmExternals: true,
        isrMemoryCacheSize: 52428800,
        concurrentFeatures: false,
        serverComponents: false,
        fullySpecified: false,
        outputFileTracingRoot: '',
        outputStandalone: true,
      },
      configFileName: 'next.config.js',
    },
  })
  handler = nextServer.getRequestHandler()

  console.log('Listening on port', currentPort)
})
```

### Expected Behavior

I would like an option to overwrite the `compress` configuration, or to have `compress` on `true` by default

### To Reproduce

Put this in `next.config.js`:
```javascript
experimental: {
    outputStandalone: true,
},
```
Execute `npm run build`, and check the `server.js` file in `.next/standalone`

Windows

Vercel

For a URL like https://vercel.com/changelog/[item] to a page `/changelog/[item].tsx`, `useRouter().query` returns an empty object `{}`.

title: uncaughtException: TypeError: res.once is not a function
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: arm64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Mon Aug 30 06:12:20 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.6~3/RELEASE_ARM64_T8101
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.2
      npm: 8.1.2
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.9
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2


### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.9

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.2

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS(Apple Silicon)

### How are you deploying your application?

next start, but this is occurred local.

### Describe the Bug

I use custom server only local to use https.
I upgraded Next.js from 11.1.2 to 12.0.9.
Then, when I reload once, browser no longer finishes loading.
And I got this error.
```
error - uncaughtException: TypeError: res.once is not a function
```


### Expected Behavior

To finish reloading with no error.

### To Reproduce

1. Set `server.js`, `./certificates/default.key` and `./certificates/default.crt`
2. Change package.json
```
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "node ./server.js",
    ...
  },
```
3. `yarn dev`
4. Load browser at the first time is success( but error has appeared )
5. Reload browser
6. never ending reload with error

This is my server.js.
```.js
const { createServer } = require('https')
const { parse } = require('url')
const next = require('next')
const fs = require('fs')

const dev = process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'
const app = next({ dev })
const handle = app.getRequestHandler()

const httpsOptions = {
    key: fs.readFileSync('./certificates/default.key'),
    cert: fs.readFileSync('./certificates/default.crt')
}

app.prepare().then(() => {
    createServer(httpsOptions, (req, res) => {
        const parsedUrl = parse(req.url, true)
        handle(req, res, parsedUrl)

    }).listen(3000, err => {
        if (err) throw err
    })
})
```


# Additional Context
it works on Next.js 11.1.2.
So, `./certificates/default.key` and `./certificates/default.crt` are no problem.
I need to use https at local because I develop PWA with next-pwa 5.4.4.

I think probably RequestHandler become not to be able to handle `res` of argument of callback function of `createServer` of `https` on Next.js 12?
Or this may be related https://nextjs.org/docs/upgrading#nextjs-hmr-connection-now-uses-a-websocket.

body:

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:29:10 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T8101
    Binaries:
      Node: 14.18.3

      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.3

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

CssSyntax error: Selector "&:hover" is not pure (pure selectors must contain at least one local class or id) (4:3)

```css
color: pink;

  &:hover {
    color: purple; 
}
```

The above repository is based on following the examples in the documentation:
https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/built-in-css-support#adding-component-level-css

This issue has been opened again since the [original one](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/10475) has been closed for "no activities" while last comment was posted 3 months ago. The issue still exists.


### Expected Behavior

As far as I understand, the above selector does contain a "pure selector", so it should work. However, apparently, since hover is nested in this example, it isn't recognized as a "pure selector".

### To Reproduce

https://github.com/andrasna/hello-next-css-module-issue

title: Static site generation doesn't recognise correctly /{locale}/{locale} routes
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

 Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: Ubuntu 20.4.0 LTS Thu Jan 27 2022 16:14:22 GMT+0100 (Central European Standard Time)
    Binaries:

      npm: 7.17.0
      Yarn: 1.22.10
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.9
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2


### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.9

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.16.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Linux/MacOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

I have an app that uses one locale for i18n, and then a two letter country code. Example URL: `/de/de`. 
The setup works correctly for routes where the country code is not one of the locales (e.g `/en/fr`) but fails for all country codes that are also locales (e.g. `/en/de`). 
It fails only for statically generated pages (with `getStaticPaths/Props` or without), server side rendered pages work correctly. 

### Expected Behavior

The app renders correctly statically generated pages for routes with two locales, the same way it does for server side rendered pages.

### To Reproduce

Link to the example code: https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-vc5urc-g5bzdn

1. Start the app with command `npm install && npx next dev`
2. Go to the path `/en/de/ssr` - works correctly
3. Go to the path `/en/de/static` - throws an error
4. Go to the path `/en/de/static-props` - page not found
5. Go to the path `/en/fr/static`- works correctly
6. Go to the path `/en/fr/static-props`- works correctly

author: humble-barnacle001

title: Fast Refresh support with preserved local state for coffeescript
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

You can add a `coffee-loader` to the `webpack` part of `next.config.js` and Fast refresh works okey, but local state inside components are lost. It would be nice if the local state could be kept.

I realise this will be so down prioritised, it would never happen, but could you give me a tip for how to implement it myself? See the technical details of the problem below.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Local state inside components being kept in coffeescript file during Fast Refresh.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Given a basic setup like so:

	config.module.rules.push({
		test: /\.(coffee)$/,
		include: [dir],
		exclude: /node_modules/,
		use: [
			defaultLoaders.babel,
			{
				loader: 'coffee-loader',
				options: {
					bare: true,
				}
			},
		],
	})

coffee-loader will compile to "fancy" js and `defaultLoaders.babel` will compile to "basic" js + **TRY TO** add the fast refresh stuff like

```
var _c, _c1;
$RefreshReg$(_c, "Home");
$RefreshReg$(_c1, "Hello");
```

But it will fail, and it seems it's because it's looking for a pattern of a functional component like so:
```
var Hello = function() {...
```
and coffeescript moves all `var`s to the top of the file while compiling like so:

```
var Hello, OtherComponent;
Hello = function Hello() { ...
```
and thus the pattern is not matched.

If we manually "improve" the output from the coffeescript compiler (before piping it in to the defaultLoaders.babel loader) by adding back the `var` like so :
```
var OtherComponent;
var Hello = function() {...
```
the `$RefreshReg$(_c1, "Hello");` gets added and Fast Refresh works with preserving the local state.

Is there any way to configure the `defaultLoaders.babel` to recognise patterns without the `var` as a component in order to add the  `$RefreshReg$(_c1, "Hello");` ?

body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

 Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.1.0: Wed Oct 13 17:33:23 PDT 2021; root:xnu-8019.41.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:

      next: 12.0.9
      react: 18.0.0-rc.0-next-fe905f152-20220107
      react-dom: 18.0.0-rc.0-next-fe905f152-20220107

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.9

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next export

### Describe the Bug

My understanding is that nextjs doesnt allow importing of static assets from global css. The docs say to put assets into a public dir and reference from there.

Because of reasons, the above solution isnt an option for me and i need to be able to reference imported static assets relatively from my css files.

My attempt at configuring webpack to allow asset rebasing results in a the assets paths in my css being prepended with `webpack:///mini-css-extract-plugin/`

Something like the below is output:

`url(webpack:///mini-css-extract-plugin/5fSGMdx82yH3OK2WXjGYzS/_next/static/media/better-times.84838048.woff2)`

The custom webpack config in my `next.js.config` looks like this:

```
const config = {
  reactStrictMode: true,
  experimental: { externalDir: true, topLevelAwait: true },
  webpack(config, options) {
    config.module.rules.push({
      test: /\.(png|jpg|gif|svg|eot|ttf|woff|woff2)$/,
      use: [
        {
          loader: 'url-loader',
          options: {
            limit: 100000
          }
        }
      ]
    })
    return config
  }
}
```

### Expected Behavior

Assets are copied to the build directory and correctly referenced from css files.

### To Reproduce

1: Create a css file that references an asset in your project using a relative path: ie `background: url('../../my-bg.jpg')`
2: Import that css in your _app.js
3: Run next build
4: Run next export

title: Folder with same basepath should be ignored in route
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

I have a next js application that I want to deploy for my documentation. This application will be hosted at mywebsite.io/docs. 

However, I have a section dedicated to my API and want to access this documentation with the url /docs/api. The problem is that if /docs is the basePath of my next js application, the actual folder structure puts my api folder at the root resulting in a never ending server call. I would love to be able to call my /docs/api page from the frontend.

### Describe the solution you'd like

The solution that I see is to have a boolean that helps us ignore the basePath folder so that it doesn't create a nested route and we can actually call the api folder

base path: /docs
folder structure: 
-pages
----docs
--------api
------------index.mdx

The normal behaviour would be to call /docs/docs/api, but with the boolean we could call /docs/api and resolve to the frontend call.




### Describe alternatives you've considered

changing my api folder name to something else or nest it inside an other folder.
author: sebastienlabine

title: Client bundle contains server code
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) ``` Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: arm64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:41 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 Binaries: Node: 16.13.1 npm: 8.1.2 Yarn: 1.22.17 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.9-canary.11 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ``` ### What version of Next.js are you using? `12.0.9-canary.11` ### What version of Node.js are you using? `16.13.1` ### What browser are you using? `Chrome` ### What operating system are you using? `macOS` ### How are you deploying your application? `yarn next build` ### Describe the Bug Client bundle contains server code. ### Expected Behavior Client bundle should not contain server code. In this case bundled `crypto` module ### To Reproduce 1) create file `/utils/jwt.ts` ``` import crypto from 'crypto' // client without 'crypto' const decode = (token: string) => JSON.parse(atob(token.split('.')[1])) // server with 'crypto' const sign = (payload: string) => crypto.createHmac('sha256', 'secret').update(payload).digest('base64') export { sign, decode } ``` 2) create file `/pages/index.tsx` ``` import { decode } from '../utils/jwt' const Home = () => { const [value, setValue] = useState(``) useState(() => setValue(decode('something')), []) return

Decoded: {value}

} export default Home ``` 3) Analyze client bundle with `@next/bundle-analyzer` 4) The client bundle contains `node_modules/next/dist/compiled/crypto-browserify`, which is way bigger than anything else. Removing the function from the file and inlining it to `index.tsx` makes the bundle small again

16.13.2

N/A

### Describe the Bug

Using `next@12.0.7` yields no invalid HTML errors on the [W3C Markup Validation Service](https://validator.w3.org/) about `next/image`.

After upgrading to `next@12.0.9`, there is now this kind of error:

```txt
Error: Bad value data:image/svg+xml,%3csvg xmlns=%27http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%27 version=%271.1%27 width=%271000%27 height=%271000%27/%3e 
for attribute src on element img: Illegal character in scheme data: space is not allowed.
```

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25207499/151701974-9545dfdc-b27b-47a4-91db-69cdb0849155.png)

There was already an error similar to this one before but that has been fixed: #18850
We might add a new e2e test or CI job to ensure that next produces valid HTML according to W3C, so it's not happening again as it is the case now...

### Expected Behavior

No errors. Valid HTML according to W3C.

### To Reproduce

1. `npx create-next-app --example image-component image-app`
2. `cd image-app`
3. `npm run build`
4. `npm run start`
5. Go to `localhost:3000` and copy/paste the HTML in [The W3C Markup Validation Service](https://validator.w3.org/) or use the [
html-w3c-validator CLI](https://github.com/Divlo/html-w3c-validator).

author: alessio-zamparelli

title: Customization options for screen-reader announcement on route-change
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

[Currently](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/3ceb9c567339e50fc6326a228841615a9a9edeac/packages/next/client/route-announcer.tsx#L12-L27), the `document.title` is given highest priority for screen-reader announcement upon route-change. That's reasonable, but it would be better for accessibility to give even higher priority to something else (something opt-in), so that developers are able to decouple the `document.title` from what screen-readers announce.

The reason (or _a_ reason, anyway) is that it's a common pattern to have the `document.title` include the site name, and if the site name is long then announcing it in full with every route-change is likely to annoy people using screen-readers. Think of an organization with a long official name and a shorter version that's typically used in communications—might be a good choice to use the full official name in the `document.title` but have screen-readers announce the short version instead.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Perhaps _highest_ priority could be given to an optional `data-sr-title` attribute on the `<title>` tag, and then the `document.title` could be used as the first back-up. Within a [`<Head>`](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/head) component, then, one might have:

```js
<title
  data-sr-title="Page Title | Short Site Title"
>Page Title | Full Site Title Is Long and We Don't Want to Force Screen-Readers to Announce the Whole Thing</title>
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Maybe the `<Head>` component could accept a prop for this purpose instead.

title: Redirects don't work inside rewritten pages
body: ### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) ``` Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: x64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64 Binaries: Node: 14.17.0 npm: 7.16.0 Yarn: 1.22.10 pnpm: 4.14.4 Relevant packages: next: 12.0.8 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ``` ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.8 ### What version of Node.js are you using? v14.17.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome, Safari, Firefox ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? Vercel ### Describe the Bug URL's which have redirects inside `next.config.js` return as 404 inside of rewritten pages ### Expected Behavior Our website has a redirect block in our `next.config.js` which is ```ts { source: "/signup", destination: "https://system.privco.com/signup", permanent: true, } ``` We also have a rewrite block ```ts async rewrites() { return [ { source: "/company/:path*", destination: `https://system.privco.com/company/:path*`, }, ... ] } ``` When inside of a rewritten page, and attempting to click the a link pointing to `/signup` we'd expect that the prefetch would follow the redirect (which works find when cURL'd or visited directly). Instead, the `next/link` prefetch returns a 404. I can see that there is an `x-vercel-status: 308` which doesn't seem to be getting honored by the prefetch with the link. The result of this is that I cannot click any link which hits a redirect on the root domain, when being navigated to from the page with the rewrite. My assumption is that if they `x-vercel-status` header was somehow parsed or honored, this issue might be fixed ### To Reproduce Visit one of our pages like `https://privco.com/company/airbnb` which is being rewritten from `https://system.privco.com/company/airbnb` and try to click on the `Sign up` or `Sign in` button.

body:

```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.2

### What browser are you using?

Google Chrome Version 99.0.4844.11 (Official Build) dev (64-bit)

### What operating system are you using?

Linux (Arch)

### How are you deploying your application?

next start | digital ocean

### Describe the Bug

1) Typescript error when dynamically import named component (see ***To Reproduce*** section for codesandbox and instructions)
2) When exporting component as a dynamic module
```javascript
const Component = () => {
  return (
    ...
  );
};
export default dynamic(() => Promise.resolve(Component), { ssr: true }); 
```
you get `Did not expect server HTML to contain a <section> in <div>` warning in console **if and only if `ssr` property is set to `true`**.
3) When dynamically imported component is used with dynamically exported component in the same component, another warning occurs: `Text content did not match. Server: "Hero header" Client: "New Hero header"`

### Expected Behavior

1) When dynamically importing named component, type checking does not result in error
2) Component dynamically exported with `ssr` set to `true` does not give a warning
3) Don't know if its legal to use dynamic import with dynamically exported component in one place. If it is, it should give a warning.

### To Reproduce

https://codesandbox.io/s/beautiful-proskuriakova-s1lii?file=/pages/index.tsx

1) Open `about.tsx` file in `pages` folder to see typescript error.
2) Open `index.tsx` file and corresponding page in browser, open Console you will see a warning: `Did not expect server HTML to contain a <section> in <div>.`
3) Open `furthermore.tsx` file and corresponding page in browser, in Console you will see warning `Text content did not match. Server: "Hero header" Client: "New Hero header"`

Steps to reproduce problem №1: 
1) Create a component that uses named export: 
```javascript
export const Component = () => { ... };
```
2) Using `dynamic`, import that component:
```javascript
const Component = dynamic(() => import("../components/Component").then(module => module.Component);
```
3) You will see Typescript doesn't like `Component`.

Steps to reproduce problem №2:
1) Create a component:
```javascript
const Component = () => { ... };
```
2) Export it using `dynamic`:
```javascript
export default dynamic(() => Promise.resolve(Component), { ssr: true }); // `srr` MUST BE SET TO `true` TO OCCUR!
```
3) Use the `Component` somewhere:
```javascript
import Component from "../Component";

const AnotherComponent = () => {
  return (
    <Component />
  )
};
```
4) Open `AnotherComponent` in a browser, open the console, see the error.

Steps to reproduce problem №3:
1) Use component from problem №1 somewhere in `AnotherComponent` from problem №2 - basically you need to have component imported dynamically `dynamic(() => import(...))` and component exported dynamically for error to occur
2) See warning in console
author: b-novikov-ipersonality

title: next/image should support running behind a proxy
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

With the recent introduction of HTTP agent config through the very beneficial `keepAlive` (https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/disabling-http-keep-alive) being enabled by default Next.js is starting to become opinionated about how fetch agents should work as a default.

With this in mind I think it would also be a good time to **introduce proxy configuration options to allow `next/image` (and other future framework layer HTTP requests) to be ran from behind a proxy.**

The primary use-case is for applications forced to run behind an egress proxy to validate outgoing requests.

Issues/discussions describing the same problem:
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/20837
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/18376
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/21874

### Describe the solution you'd like

## Solution 1 (Preferred)

Use the widely accepted HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY variables when configuring the fetch agent.

## Solution 2

An extension of the existing `httpAgentOptions` to include a proxy configuration, e.g in `next.config.js`

```js
module.exports = {
  httpAgentOptions: {
    keepAlive: false,
    proxy: {
        protocol: 'https',
        host: 'https://my-proxy-address.com',
        port: 443
  },
}
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

An alternative solution is [to override the HTTP agent globally like described here](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/20837) but with this solution it will also [overwrite the keep alive that the Next.js framework introduces with great benefit](https://github.com/gajus/global-agent#what-is-the-reason-global-agent-overrides-explicitly-configured-https-agent).
Without access to the framework I don't see how we can merge agent configs on a per-option basis in a good way, hence the global overwriting, resulting in overriding beneficial behaviours.

error Command failed with exit code 1.
info Visit https://yarnpkg.com/en/docs/cli/run for documentation about this command.
```

```
$ cat /etc/redhat-release
CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)

$ yum list installed | grep glibc
glibc.x86_64                            2.17-325.el7_9               @updates   
glibc-common.x86_64                     2.17-325.el7_9               @updates   
glibc-devel.x86_64                      2.17-325.el7_9               @updates   
glibc-headers.x86_64                    2.17-325.el7_9               @updates

### Expected Behavior

Execution of `next build` succeeds even if GLIBC < 2.18.


### To Reproduce

Run `next build` on CentOS 7 Docker image

$ docker pull centos:7
$ docker run --rm -it centos:7 /bin/bash

in docker

```

author: aboda1986

title: Add DOM attribute when next/image is done loading
body: ### Describe the feature you'd like to request Hi there! I run a screenshot testing service called happo.io (proudly run on next.js since 2017). Lately I've had to debug a few issues with spurious screenshots coming from images constructed via `next/image`, for [Cypress test runs](https://docs.happo.io/docs/cypress). For screenshot testing to be stable, it's important that we know when images are fully loaded. With `next/image`, it's hard to do that without either: - inspecting the style attribute to look for `/filter.*blur/ - add a `load` event listener for the image element in user land - poll images for the `complete` property to be `true` To be clear, this is mostly a Cypress/e2e testing issue where Happo (and other services, like Percy) take a DOM snapshot of the page/element before sending that DOM snapshot off to a screenshot service. We need to know that the DOM is fully ready. We could add some code that will always wait for images to be downloaded before taking the DOM snapshot, but that would make a number of test suites slower, and we don't want that. We just need the `` element to be populated with enough information so that we can recreate it later. In the case of `next/image`, we need to wait for the DOM to be in the "ready" state -- no blurry background image. ### Describe the solution you'd like It would be super helpful if the underlying `img` element got a special attribute/class name when we know it's fully loaded. Something like ```html ``` Or perhaps the image can be in different states: ```html ``` ### Describe alternatives you've considered This is possible to solve in user land by adding something like ```js cy.get('img').should($imgs => $imgs.map((_i, img) => expect(img.complete).to.be.true) ) ```

title: Docs: Add whether Next Export compatible for each feature
body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

> _Note: this is a general issue which applies to multiple pages in the Next.js documentation_

Currently, the Next docs _sometimes_ specify whether something works for `next export`, but sometimes they do not.

For every docs page specifying a major Next.js feature, it would be useful to see whether that feature is compatible with Next when exported as a static site.

### Is there any context that might help us understand?

Overall, I find that Next.js's docs are really explanatory, but they're written (understandably) from the standpoint of running a Next app, as a dynamic server that accepts requests and responses.

With the proliferation of static sites, a lesser-used but equally important area to document would be whether each feature is compatible with `next export`.

For instance, I was looking at the [multi-zones page](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/advanced-features/multi-zones.md), and it makes no specific mention of use with `next build` vs `next export`.

The `next/image` page on the other hand [_does_ mention a specific caveat](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/image#built-in-loaders) with regard to loader use in `next export`.

It would be great to have a statement about `next export` compatibility on every docs page that relates to a major feature, especially advanced features and the API reference pages.

### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

_No response_

### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up) Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: x64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64 Binaries: Node: 16.13.0 npm: 8.1.0 Yarn: 1.22.0 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.10 react: 0.0.0-experimental-fa816be7f-20220128 react-dom: 0.0.0-experimental-fa816be7f-20220128 ### What version of Next.js are you using? 12.0.10 ### What version of Node.js are you using? 16.13.0 ### What browser are you using? Chrome ### What operating system are you using? macOS ### How are you deploying your application? next start ### Describe the Bug @devknoll asked me to file an issue for this. Basically, I'm observing that with React experimental versions, SSG doesn't wait for `lazy()` to be resolved and outputs the Suspense fallback HTML. Whereas I would've expected it to include content in SSG. I'm not sure if this means `import()` needs to be compiled differently, or something. A similar issue is that in SSR mode, the first hit will get fallback HTML and the next hits will get content HTML. Because the module is cached now. ### Expected Behavior HTML output of SSG includes the SSR content of the lazy child. ### To Reproduce Create a new clean project. Add experimental React versions to it. Page code: ```js import {lazy, Suspense} from 'react' import Head from 'next/head' const Child = lazy(() => import('../components/Child')) export default function Home() { return (
Create Next App

Welcome to Next.js!

Loading...

}>
) } ``` Component code: ```js // Some heavy lib to simulate loading a large chunk import {marked} from 'marked' export default function Child() { return
} ``` Run `yarn build` and then `yarn start`. Observe that the served HTML does **not** include `hello, world!` but does include `Loading...`.

title: optimizeCss/critters causing flash of unstyled text
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: arm64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.3.0: Wed Jan  5 21:37:58 PST 2022; root:xnu-8019.80.24~20/RELEASE_ARM64_T8101
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.1
      npm: 8.1.2
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.9
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.9

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS 12.2

### How are you deploying your application?

next start (custom server)

### Describe the Bug

Next's use of `critters` is configured with the `fonts` flag set to `false`. `fonts` is a shorthand for `preloadFonts` and `inlineFonts`, which tells `critters`:

> don't inline any font-face rules and don't preload fonts

Not inlining the font-face will cause a short flash of unstyled text because the page is rendered and painted before the deferred "non-critical" CSS file is downloaded. And that file contains the font-face declaration. 

### Process:
1. HTML document with inlined CSS is downloaded.
2. All the `font-family` values refer to undeclared fonts, e.g. `My Font, sans-serif`.
3. Page is laid out with fallback font, `sans-serif` in this example.
4. At some point, the deferred non-critical CSS file containing `@font-face` declarations loads.
5. Page is repainted with font information (flash!).

### Workaround:
We've moved our `@font-face` declarations from our SCSS files to a `<style>` element in `_app.tsx`. 

### Solutions?
1. I am not sure of the implications, but the `critters` config could be changed from:

```
const cssOptimizer = new Critters({
  ssrMode: true,
  reduceInlineStyles: false,
  path: renderOpts.distDir,
  publicPath: `${renderOpts.assetPrefix}/_next/`,
  preload: 'media',
  fonts: false,  <--- this could be changed to...
  ...renderOpts.optimizeCss,
})
```

to 

```
const cssOptimizer = new Critters({
  ssrMode: true,
  reduceInlineStyles: false,
  path: renderOpts.distDir,
  publicPath: `${renderOpts.assetPrefix}/_next/`,
  preload: 'media',
  preloadFonts: false,  <--- this 
  inlineFonts: true, <--- and this
  ...renderOpts.optimizeCss,
})
```

2. Let users pass  options to `critters` in `next.config`:
```
{
  experimental: {
    optimizeCss: {
      inlineFonts: true
    }
  }
}
```

(I am aware that `optimizeCss` is experimental, but filing this issue is OK, right?) 

### Expected Behavior

I expect no flash of unstyled text, meaning I expect the font-face declaration to be included in critical CSS. 

### To Reproduce

### Site with
- Custom `@font-face` in globals.css
- `experimental.optimizeCss: true` 

https://next-flash-issue-repro.vercel.app/
https://github.com/madsfaerch/next-flash-issue-repro

Notice the flash when refreshing (disable cache when refreshing)

### Site with
- Custom `@font-face` in `<style />` in `<head />` (_app.js).
- `experimental.optimizeCss: true` 

https://next-flash-issue-with-fix.vercel.app/
https://github.com/madsfaerch/next-flash-issue-with-fix

Notice that there's no flash.

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

We recently attempted to upgrade to Next v12.0.8 from 11.x.x and we are no longer able to build the application and get the following error:

```JSX
Module not found: Can't resolve 'process'
> 25 | {process.env.COMPANY} Technology, Inc.
```

In our application, we access our build time environment variable in our components with `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_`. In 11.x.x we didn't have any issues and we are able to reference the values. 

Currently, we don't have anything set in `next.config.js` because we didn't need to have any configurations set to access these variables previously. If I install `process` as a dependency, then everything works as expected.

I noticed in v12.0.8 `process` was removed as a dependency in a PR to pre-compile more dependencies:
https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/32627

Is there something that needs to happen on our end for `process` to be accessible?

I've seen someone else state the same issue as well here:
https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/33469#issuecomment-1018547259



### Expected Behavior

No errors when resolving modules.

### To Reproduce

I'm having issues getting environments set up to provide a reproducible example but I will keep trying to get something set up.

body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

 Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: arm64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.4.0: Tue Jan 18 13:02:08 PST 2022; root:xnu-8020.100.406.0.1~18/RELEASE_ARM64_T8101
    Binaries:

    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.2

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Docker

### Describe the Bug

I have noticed the `outputStandalone` option and decide to try it for our docker deployment. It worked great and dramatically reduced the size of the image (x12). However, I stumbled upon a small problem. 

When the server.js file is generated, it automatically expands `publicRuntimeConfig` and `serverRuntimeConfig` into the conf entry of NextServer. This pretty much means that we cannot use any dynamic environment variables in docker, as all of them are only respected during build.

### Expected Behavior

next.config.js should be copied to the standalone output and its configuration should be used.

It's quite common to have a single docker image that can suit all environments with different configuration. Using the regular next build, without `outputStandalone`, this is also possible.

### To Reproduce

Add the following to next.config.js:
```
    serverRuntimeConfig: {
      API_URL: process.env.API_URL
    },
    experimental: {
      outputStandalone: true
    },
```

run `next build`

inspect `.next/standalone/server.js`- there is no API_URL entry in `serverRuntimeConfig`

```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.11-canary.1

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Unrelated

### What operating system are you using?

Windows 11

### How are you deploying your application?

Unrelated

### Describe the Bug

When a dependency package has `"type": "module"` in package.json, and uses `new Worker(new URL("./worker.js", import.meta.url))` to create a web worker, `next build` failed with

```
Module not found: ESM packages (./worker.js) need to be imported. Use 'import' to reference the package instead. https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/import-esm-externals
```

While `next dev` and a barebone webpack configuration all works.

---

This issue originated near here:

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/5365f417853e11fb000ad95729d9f9b68d2a3e42/packages/next/build/webpack-config.ts#L849-L851

`dependencyType` is `"worker"` in this case.

### Expected Behavior

`next build` successfully create a build.

### To Reproduce

```
git clone https://github.com/yume-chan/next-repro-33914
cd next-import-worker
npm i
cd packages/next-app
next build
```

The `webpack-app` folder is a barebone webpack configuration, which can correctly import and bundle the worker.

body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

Operating System:

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOs

### How are you deploying your application?

with Docker

### Describe the Bug

The project worked perfectly with Next 9.5.3

There are .sass files with provided styles for background images:

`background: url('data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D%22ht...`

But, after updating to Next 12.0.9 / 12.0.10, replacing packages as it is described in migration guide there are now Errors during compilation:
Where [pathToMyFile].module.sass - is the same file.

```
error - ./node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/css-loader/src/index.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[1]!./node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/postcss-loader/src/index.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[2]!./node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/resolve-url-loader/index.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[3]!./node_modules/next/dist/compiled/sass-loader/cjs.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[4]!./[pathToMyFile].module.sass
frontend_1   | URIError: URI malformed
frontend_1   |     at decodeURIComponent (<anonymous>)
```

```
<w> while serializing webpack/lib/cache/PackFileCacheStrategy.PackContentItems -> webpack/lib/NormalModule -> webpack/lib/ModuleBuildError -> URIError
frontend_1   | (node:48) [DEP_WEBPACK_MODULE_ISSUER] DeprecationWarning: Module.issuer: Use new ModuleGraph API
frontend_1   | (Use `node --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created)
<w> [webpack.cache.PackFileCacheStrategy] Skipped not serializable cache item 'Compilation/modules|/frontend/node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/css-loader/src/index.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[1]!/frontend/node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/postcss-loader/src/index.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[2]!/frontend/node_modules/next/dist/build/webpack/loaders/resolve-url-loader/index.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[3]!/frontend/node_modules/next/dist/compiled/sass-loader/cjs.js??ruleSet[1].rules[2].oneOf[5].use[4]!/frontend/[pathToMyFile].module.sass': No serializer registered for URIError
```


### Expected Behavior

Parsing css background property with data-url

### To Reproduce

I have updated to Next 12.0.10, replace @zeit/next-sass, node-sass with "sass" package. Everything as provided in migration guide. Removed url-loader package and in next.config.js I have replaced the rule:

```
config.module.rules = config.module.rules.concat([
      {
        test: /\.(png|jpg|gif|svg|eot|ttf|woff|woff2)$/,
        use: [
          {
            loader: 'url-loader',
            options: {
              limit: 4096,
              fallback: 'file-loader'
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    ])
```

**with this configuration:** 
```
config.module.rules = config.module.rules.concat([
      {
        test: /\.(png|jpg|gif|svg|eot|ttf|woff|woff2)$/,
        type: 'asset'
      }
    ])
```

Am I missing something in configuration?

Because I found out, that if I replace background property with background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml,%3Cs...') there is no error in console. But there are a lot of places where we are using background prop with data url. 

It should work and compile correctly as in Next 9.5.3 version.
author: gritaliana

title: Application should render before Document when streaming is turned on
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

 Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.3.0: Wed Jan  5 21:37:58 PST 2022; root:xnu-8019.80.24~20/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.0.0
      npm: 7.10.0
      Yarn: 1.22.11
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.8
      react: 18.0.0-rc.0
      react-dom: 18.0.0-rc.0

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.8

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.0.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

[Document renders](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/f104e9105c929c343369747644a780d67171b557/packages/next/server/render.tsx#L1407) before [application renders](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/f104e9105c929c343369747644a780d67171b557/packages/next/server/render.tsx#L1448) when streaming is turned on (i.e. concurrentFeatures flag is set to true), so it builds the `<head>` tag before next/head instances are discovered in the component tree. As a result, next/head tags are not in the `<head>` of the initial SSR response and only are added to `<head>` once the component tree renders on the client side. This is an invisible performance regression if there are resource tags in next/head, since it appears to work but the tags load much later in the process.

cc @shuding @huozhi @devknoll 

### Expected Behavior

Application should render before the Document so next/head instances are discovered before the `<head>` tag is built. This would prevent the performance regression.

### To Reproduce

1. Have a component that uses next/head (no Suspense boundaries):

```
function Profile(props) {
   return (
       <Head> 
          <link rel="stylesheet" ... />
        </Head>
   );
}
```

2. Turn on `concurrentFeatures` flag 
3.  Look at network response for the document and see that stylesheet isn't in the `<head>` tag

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.2

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

`rewrites()` in `next.config.js` is only called at build time.
IMHO it should be called at run time.

*Use Case:* If I don't know beforehand the url to where I want to rewrite I can't define it at runtime from an Environment variable.

### Expected Behavior

`rewrites()` should be called at runtime so that we can have a dynamic rewrite url using an env variable.

### To Reproduce

put a log in `rewrites()` in `next.config.js` and verify that it is only called at build time and not at runtime.

title: Absolute links generated during ISR get prefixed with the i18n domain if a Host header is provided
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

/bin/sh: pnpm: command not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Wed Nov 10 22:23:07 PST 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.14~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.10.0
      npm: 7.24.0
      Yarn: 1.22.5
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
✨  Done in 1.05s.

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.10.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome and curl

### What operating system are you using?

macOS and in Docker container

### How are you deploying your application?

next start and Docker container with next start

### Describe the Bug

Given the following:
1. i18n domains setup in `next.config.js` e.g. `example.nl`
2. ISR setup of a dynamic page (not sure it has to be a dynamic page); e.g. `revalidate: 1`
3. An absolute link rendered with `next/link` e.g. `<Link href="https://nextjs.org">Test</Link>`

When the page triggers a new build with ISR from a request that has a Host header for the domain configured in the i18n domains config, the href will be prefixed with that domain. To summarize the reproduction steps: `curl -H "Host: example.nl" http://localhost:3000/posts/pre-rendering` turns the `https://nextjs.org` link href into `https://example.nlhttps://nextjs.org`.

### Expected Behavior

No domain prefixing of absolute URLs (also what is the point of domain prefixing at all?)

### To Reproduce

https://github.com/MartijnHols/nextjs-absolute-links-bug

README with reproduction steps and clean commits to see the setup needed.
