title: Middleware Use Breaks Locale Routing on `/` Path on Vercel if autodetected locale is not default locale
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
/bin/sh: pnpm: command not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.5.0: Sat May  8 05:10:33 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.121.3~9/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 14.17.5
      npm: 6.14.14
      Yarn: 1.22.5
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.17.5

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

When using automatic locale detection, and the detected locale is NOT the default locale, trying to route to another locale on `/` results in a failed prefetch error and doesn't complete routing.

In my case my locales are `["ja","en"]` and my defaultLocale is `ja`. My browser language is set to english, and with the automatic locale detection, I'm taken to the `/en` route. When I try to route to the `ja` locale in `yarn dev` or `yarn build && yarn start` everything works fine. However when I deploy to Vercel I get:

```
Uncaught (in promise) Error: Failed to preflight request
```
And routing is cancelled. 

My Japanese users with a japanese langauge setting have no issue moving between the two locales. and if I disable the automatic locale detection I will be taken to `/` with a `ja` locale as expected, and I can freely route between the two locales. 

### Expected Behavior

I'm expecting to not get a preflight failure from Vercel, and routing to complete successfully.

This might be related to https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/34199

### To Reproduce

I've created a minimum reproducable repo here using `create-next-app` and the official example at: https://github.com/vercel/examples/tree/main/edge-functions/basic-auth-password

Repo/Commits on Github: https://github.com/sollective/next-locale-bug-repro/commits/main

I also have it hosted on Vercel here: 
https://next-locale-bug-repro.vercel.app/en (Credentials are `admin`/`password`)
(Please set your primary language to English in browser)

title: routeChangeComplete event is emitted for initial page load when fallback rewrite is enabled
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.0
      npm: 8.1.0
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.0

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS Monterey 12.1

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

In a fresh Next project, the router does not emit a `routeChangeComplete` event for the initial page load, as documented elsewhere:

```
    router.events.on('routeChangeComplete', onRouteChangeComplete);
```

But if you modify the `next.config.js` to include a fallback rewrite, the router DOES emit a `routeChangeComplete` event for the initial page load:

```
  rewrites: async () => ({
    fallback: [
      {
        source: '/:path*',
        destination: `http://localhost:4444/:path*`,
      },
    ],
  }),
```

This behavior is inconsistent and makes it difficult to know when to expect the "extra" initial event for analytics purposes.

### Expected Behavior

I would like the router events to behave consistently and intuitively. Personally I would prefer if the initial page load always emitted the event, but ultimately I just want it to be consistent so we're not surprised by the behavior.

### To Reproduce

I have created a fresh NextJS 12 TypeScript repo and modified it to demonstrate the behavior: https://github.com/lafiosca/nextjs-initial-route-example

The changes I made are simple: https://github.com/lafiosca/nextjs-initial-route-example/commit/290e37224ebc2bf5f6e8f7fcda30be66dd3221f1

I configured a catch-all fallback rewrite to an arbitrary host, as described in the Incremental Adoption example here: https://nextjs.org/docs/migrating/incremental-adoption

And then I added a listener for `routeChangeComplete` in `_app.tsx` which outputs the url.

I ran the project in local dev mode, opened it in Chrome, and viewed the console:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9442662/153725563-0c7dd187-f4d6-46cd-bfd1-361ff0c2713a.png)

If you comment out the single entry from the `fallback` config and restart the server:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9442662/153725416-528a54f9-886d-4e0a-a9f6-c7a276083f54.png)

You'll see a different behavior:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9442662/153725440-8a3640b5-ffeb-4952-b4bd-94fdc14c1245.png)

title: Dynamically required files (seemingly) no longer run on the same process
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: arm64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:41 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.1
      npm: 8.1.2
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.3.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

When dynamically requiring/importing files, the source code seems to run on different a process. Suppose if you declare a global variable and check its value, the one imported into the "main process" will be different from the one imported into the dynamically required file.

By dynamically requiring files, I am referring to either of these approaches:
```js
require(`./path/to/${fileName}`)
await import(`./path/to/${fileName}`)
```

#### Concept
1. First, declare a `new Date().toISOString()` global variable in a `constants.ts` file.
2. Then import it into say, one of the files in `pages/api/`.
3. Then from `pages/api/`, dynamically require another file.
4. That "another file" should also import the global variable.
5. Then, log the global variable in `pages/api/` and that "another file".
6. Since it's a global variable, you'd expect it to be declared once only, but the values end up being different.

Note: This doesn't happen in v11, as far as I've noticed, it's only happening in v12.

### Expected Behavior

That global variable when logged in multiple places, should have the same value.


### To Reproduce

1. Clone [this](https://github.com/glyph-cat/nextjs-dynamic-require-inconsistency) repository.
2. Run `yarn install`.
3. Run `yarn dev`.
4. Make a GET request with this url (can be from Postman or any browser, but I used Postman):
```
http://localhost:3000/api/greet?templateName=hello&personName=John%20Doe
```
5. Compare the value of `RUNTIME_ID` from files in the `~templates` folder and in `pages/api/greet`.
6. Stop the process, run `yarn add next@11.1.4` and `yarn dev` again, then repeat steps 4 to 5.

Notes:
* React's Context API is used to demonstrate how this can actually be an issue. For example, the values passed into the Context is not received in the dynamically required templates because they seem to "run on different processes".
* This might be an edge case, but I am relying on React as a templating tool to render huge amounts of text, so whether this is really a bug or an intended feature of v12, I hope my reason for opening this issue is legitimate to some extent. `(￣▽￣)`

<hr/>

#### Example output

Expected:
```json
{
    "renderedOutput": "Hello, John Doe. (RUNTIME_ID is 2022-02-14T03:43:34.782Z)",
    "RUNTIME_ID": "2022-02-14T03:43:34.782Z"
}
```

Actual:
```json
{
    "renderedOutput": "Hello, Unknown. (RUNTIME_ID is 2022-02-14T03:43:34.789Z)",
    "RUNTIME_ID": "2022-02-14T03:43:34.782Z"
}
```

title: "Unhandled Runtime Error, incompatible-href-as" when redirecting to an absolute URL via middleware with a subdomain and fallback=true in development mode
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

 Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #1 SMP Thu, 20 Jan 2022 13:15:13 +0000
    Binaries:
      Node: 17.3.0
      npm: 8.4.0
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

17.3.0

### What browser are you using?

Chromium

### What operating system are you using?

Archlinux

### How are you deploying your application?

yarn start

### Describe the Bug

Hi, 

This issue is kinda similar with an issue I had recently #32446 and you can still relate to this code for this issue. To summarize when trying to redirect via `_middleware.tsx` and when `fallback` was set to true, when trying to visit a non generated at build time path I had the `Unhandled Runtime Error, incompatible-href-as` error. This has been fixed in a canary release. 

The fact is that I was rewriting to a relative path and it will soon be deprecated https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/middleware-relative-urls , when replacing 
```
const rewrited_path = `/_sites/${currentHost}${pathname}`;
 return NextResponse.rewrite(rewrited_path);
```

with 

```
const rewrited_path = `**${hostname}**/_sites/${currentHost}${pathname}`;
return NextResponse.rewrite(rewrited_path);
```

I got the same error as before. As before the problem only occurs with non generated at build time path and only with `fallback` set to true. But this time the problem is only occurring in development (when redirecting to http://domain-2.localhost:300/... for example) and not in production. I'm conditionally rewriting to the relative path in dev but it'll become problematic once deprecated. 

Thanks in advance.  

### Expected Behavior

No such error in dev mode. 

### To Reproduce

Follow the code of #32466 and, in `_middleware.tsx` replace the rewrite path chunk of code as described in this issue.

### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

 Operating System:

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome 98

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

The image path is wrong when the images.path, image.loader and assetPrefix is configured.

### Expected Behavior

With the following configuration:
```js
const nextConfig = {
  reactStrictMode: true,
  images: {
    loader: 'imgix',
    path: 'https://my-imgix-cdn.imgix.net'
  },
  assetPrefix: 'https://my-custom-cdn.com',
}
```

I would expect that the image path should be:
```
https://my-imgix-cdn.imgix.net/_next/static/media/150.8e513b46.png?auto=format&fit=max&w=256
```
But instead, I got:
```
https://my-imgix-cdn.imgix.net/https://my-custom-cdn.com/_next/static/media/150.8e513b46.png?auto=format&fit=max&w=256
```

### To Reproduce

1. git clone https://github.com/GiancarlosIO/nextjs-image-loader-bug
2. yarn install
3. yarn build
4. yarn start
5. Inspect the image and you'll see that the image path is `https://my-imgix-cdn.imgix.net/https://my-custom-cdn.com/_next/static/media/150.8e513b46.png?auto=format&fit=max&w=256` instead of `https://my-imgix-cdn.imgix.net/_next/static/media/150.8e513b46.png?auto=format&fit=max&w=256`

title: `rewrites.beforeFiles` should work the same with or without `i18n` configuration.
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #1 SMP PREEMPT Tue, 01 Feb 2022 21:42:50 +0000
    Binaries:
      Node: 17.3.0
      npm: 8.4.1
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.11-canary.15
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.11-canary.15

### What version of Node.js are you using?

17.3.0

### What browser are you using?

Firefox

### What operating system are you using?

Linux

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

Rewrites with `beforeFiles` does not work as it used to be with `i18n` enabled. (last known to be working in next@11.1.2)

Back in `next@11.1.2` I used to have this `rewrites` configuration:
```js
  i18n,
  async rewrites() {
    return {
      beforeFiles: [
        {
          source: "/base/:path*",
          destination: "/:path*",
        },
      ],
    };
  },
```
and my urls such as `/base/_next/static/chunks/main.js` used to work. (Use case: CDN shenanigans, CDN are configured to rewrites `assets.xxx.com/foo/*` through `foo.xxx.com/assets/*` for micro frontends.)

Upon upgrading to `next@12.0.10`, this behavior does not work anymore.

This bug does not occurs without `i18n` configured.

I'm providing a PR with tests to confirm the broken behavior, although I've simplified the use case by using an image and not compiled file. Should I add a check for compiled files also?

**Related:** https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/23270
**History:** https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/23455

### Expected Behavior

`rewrites.beforeFiles` should work the same with or without `i18n` configuration.

### To Reproduce

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/34370
author: LeonardDrs

title: Docs: Please add documentation on how to load server-side data for layouts and _app.js and _document.js
body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

A lot of websites have a layout that contains:

- Navigation
- Footer
- Sidebar
- ...

Sometimes these sections have something that is dynamic and needs to be loaded from the server.

In the documentation, it's [said](https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/layouts#data-fetching) that we should use client-side `useEffect` or `SWR` to load data for these sections.

But this approach is not good for SEO.

Please address this issue in the documentation. Please document how can we create SEO-friendly layouts, _app.js and _document.js.

Thank you.

### Is there any context that might help us understand?

We need to be able to somehow provide dynamic data for:

- Layouts
- _app.js
- _document.js

And if we can't do it, we need to have a place that explains it clearly and enumerates it as part of the problems of SEO with next.js.

### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/layouts#data-fetching

```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.0.4

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Development for know

### Describe the Bug

This is not a bug at all. But it's more currently on applications.

```
A page or script is accessing at least one of navigator.userAgent, navigator.appVersion, and navigator.platform. Starting in Chrome 101, the amount of information available in the User Agent string will be reduced.
To fix this issue, replace the usage of navigator.userAgent, navigator.appVersion, and navigator.platform with feature detection, progressive enhancement, or migrate to navigator.userAgentData.
Note that for performance reasons, only the first access to one of the properties is shown.
```

### Expected Behavior

There is information about it on https://blog.chromium.org/2021/09/user-agent-reduction-origin-trial-and-dates.html

### To Reproduce

Just building and starting the app. This is are present on `content.bundle.js:1 `and `invisible.js:1`.

The pages load when running `next dev`, but when running `next build` it gets stuck at `info  - Creating an optimized production build`. I have only updated the ui library. build to work

body:

      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.3

### What browser are you using?

Brave

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

When a middleware function redirects to an external site the query params received by the middleware are appended to the redirect target. Middleware cannot redirect to a target with no query params if the middleware received params.


### Expected Behavior

Redirects from middleware should use the redirect target specified by the middleware without modification to the query string.

### To Reproduce

For example:
- A request is made to /foo?bar=baz
- A middleware function handles the request and redirects to `https://nextjs.org`.
- The `location` header returned to the client has a value of `https://nextjs.org?bar=baz`

This is reproducible when deployed to Vercel, but not when running locally with `next dev`.

Repro deployment: https://next-middleware-redirects.vercel.app/

Code: https://github.com/bryanrsmith/next-middleware-redirects

title: _middleware breaking server in runtime
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
/bin/sh: yarn: command not found
/bin/sh: pnpm: command not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.1.0: Wed Oct 13 17:33:23 PDT 2021; root:xnu-8019.41.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 12.22.3
      npm: 6.14.13
      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v12.22.3

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

Getting http status 500 on every request when using `_middleware` feature

### Expected Behavior

Getting http status 200 on every valid request when using `_middleware` feature

### To Reproduce

1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest --ts`
2. Add file `_middleware.ts` in `pages/` with the content bellow
```
// _middleware.ts
import type { NextFetchEvent, NextRequest } from 'next/server';
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(req: NextRequest, ev: NextFetchEvent) {
    NextResponse.next();
}
```
3. Modify `next.config.js` with the content bellow
```
// next.config.js
/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */
const nextConfig = {
  reactStrictMode: true,

  webpack(config, { isServer }) {
    if (!isServer) {
      config.externals = {
          ...(config.externals || {}),
          react: 'React',
          'react-dom': 'ReactDOM',
      };
    }

    return config;
  }
}

module.exports = nextConfig
```
4. Run `npm run build` and then `npm run start`
5. Visit `localhost:3000` in browser

There are two reasons why this issue is happening. Firstly there should be some other flag besides `isServer` flag to distinguish middleware in build time (something like `target: 'server' | 'client' | 'middleware'`). Secondly 'react' dependency  is unnecessary for middleware bundle. It used only by [this file](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/694407450638b037673c6d714bfe4126aeded740/packages/next/shared/lib/utils.ts) for creating `HtmlContext`, but `HtmlContext` is not used in middleware bundle. So manually removing it from bundle after build time fixes the problem. Looks like the problem with  tree-shaking 

Linked issues: #31524 #32369

      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

Edge

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next start

### Describe the Bug

If I try to fetch data in ` middleware` function using Undici the following error occurs:

```
./node_modules/undici/lib/api/api-connect.js:4:24
Module not found: Can't resolve 'async_hooks'

Import trace for requested module:
./node_modules/undici/lib/api/index.js
./node_modules/undici/index.js
./src/pages/_middleware.ts

https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/module-not-found
```

I would expect `undici` to work as it will be the future Fetch implementation that will get bundled with Node.js: 
https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/41749

### Expected Behavior

Calling `undici.fetch()` should work

### To Reproduce

1. Create Next.js app
2. Add `_middleware.ts` to `pages`-directory
3. Add contents to `_middleware.ts`:

```typescript
/* eslint-disable no-console */
import type { NextFetchEvent, NextRequest } from 'next/server'
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
import * as undici from 'undici'

export async function middleware(req: NextRequest, ev: NextFetchEvent) {
  console.log(`middleware() req:`, req)
  console.log(`middleware() ev:`, ev)
  try {
    const response = await undici.fetch('https://www.vercel.com', { method: 'GET' })
    const json = await response.text()
    console.log(`response:`, response.status, json)
    return NextResponse.rewrite(
      '/marketing/b'
    )
  } catch (err: unknown) {
    console.log(`Error occurred:`, err)
    return NextResponse.rewrite(
      '/marketing/a'
    )
  }
}
```
4. Start server `next dev`
5. Notice the error when accessing the Next.js server

```
./node_modules/undici/lib/api/api-connect.js:4:24
Module not found: Can't resolve 'async_hooks'

Import trace for requested module:
./node_modules/undici/lib/api/index.js
./node_modules/undici/index.js
./src/pages/_middleware.ts

https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/module-not-found
```

title: iOS safari crashes when huge amount of next/image instances are rendered & user is heavily scrolling
body: ### Verify canary release - [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release ### Provide environment information Operating System: Platform: linux Arch: x64 Version: Ubuntu 20.4.0 LTS Thu Feb 17 2022 02:26:32 GMT+0100 (Mitteleuropäische Normalzeit) Binaries: Node: 14.17.0 npm: 7.17.0 Yarn: 1.22.10 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.0.11-canary.20 react: 17.0.1 react-dom: 17.0.1 ### What browser are you using? (if relevant) iOS Safari 15.3.1 ### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant) vercel ### Describe the Bug we use next/image to render a list of product images in our eCommerce platform and noticed the following problem: when the user has fetched hundreds of products and heavily scrolls up & down, then the current browser tabs crashes and get restarted automatically. When we use a normal with same srcset, sizes etc then we were not able to reproduce the issue in that case. there is no detailed crash report or stack trace visible - even a connected desktop safari (devtools, connected device) is not showing any hints. It simply prints sth like "the website was reloaded because a problem occurred" (german browser: "die Webseite wurde neu geladen, weil ein problem aufgetreten ist") I was not able to reproduce the issue by using a XCode device simulator or any desktop browser. I've attached a screencam that shows the error. https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1340740/154388757-23f7a91b-88e3-4eb9-a24a-73f40532543c.mov ### Expected Behavior browser does not crash, even with hundreds of next/images instances in the JSX/DOM tree. ### To Reproduce #### With infiniteQuery (useInfiniteQuery) I've created a stackblitz instance here: [https://stackblitz.com/edit/nextjs-oy8czv?file=pages/index.js](https://stackblitz.com/edit/nextjs-oy8czv?file=pages/index.js) this stackblitz is also added to github here: [https://github.com/alfrescian/nextjs-infinite-images](https://github.com/alfrescian/nextjs-infinite-images) and deployed to vercel here: [https://github.com/alfrescian/nextjs-infinite-images](https://nextjs-infinite-images.vercel.app/) #### even more simplified (useQuery) I've created a stackblitz instance here: [https://stackblitz.com/edit/nextjs-nx8f23?file=pages/index.js](https://stackblitz.com/edit/nextjs-nx8f23?file=pages/index.js) this stackblitz is also added to github here: [https://github.com/alfrescian/nextjs-images](https://github.com/alfrescian/nextjs-images) and deployed to vercel here: [https://nextjs-images-eight.vercel.app/](https://nextjs-images-eight.vercel.app//)

title: Loading custom font when using styled-components loads multiple times and creates short flashes
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Wed Jun 23 00:26:31 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.2~5/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.0
      npm: 8.3.0
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.11-canary.20
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

Firefox Developer 98.0b5 (64-bit) & Google Chrome Version 98.0.4758.80 (Official Build) (x86_64)

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

### Describe the Bug

# Bug report description

I am using NextJS with styled-components and try to load a custom font. I tried different implementations and all of 
them are buggy. Am I doing something wrong or is there a bug somewhere?

## Bug 1: font-face defined in GlobalStyles / styled-components  (main branch)

If I define the font-face in style/index.js, the **font gets loaded 3 times** and sometimes **there is a short flash** where 
the text disappears and appears again.

Check out this video (maybe slow it down to 0.25x): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tGsEaL8Ppvk

## Bug 2: font-face defined in global.css (css branch)

Defining everything in global.css works just fine. Also defining the font-face in global.css and assigning the 
font-family in styled components GlobalStyle works. But if I add `display: flex` or `display: grid` to the body tag in 
my global styles, the font is first displayed incorrectly and then switches to my custom font. If I use 
`display: absolute`, no problem, it works as intended.

I don't get why using flex or grid affects fonts. I did move this code in a global css in my project, so it works fine, 
but would like to find out the reason or the bug, in order to fix it or not make this mistake again in the future.

Check out this video (maybe slow it down to 0.25x): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mvxPKKQmrDA


### Expected Behavior

Bug 1: font-face definition in GlobalStyles should not trigger 3 requests to load the fonts and the page should not rerender/flash

Bug 2: using display flex or display grid should have the same behavior as display block or absolute (related to fonts). Custom font should be displayed from the beginning.

### To Reproduce

## Bug 1: font-face defined in GlobalStyles / styled-components  (main branch)

1. Clone repo https://github.com/cdelacombaz/nextjs-fonts-bugreport
2. run `yarn install`
3. Ensure that you are on **main** branch
4. run `yarn run dev`
5. visit http://localhost:3000
6. disable cache in your browser and reload the page (flash only happens sometimes)


## Bug 2: font-face defined in global.css (css branch)

(clone repo and run `yarn install` as mentioned previously)
1. Switch to **css** branch
2. run `yarn run dev`
3. visit http://localhost:3000
4. refresh the page

Chrome 98.0.4758.80 (arm64)

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

Clicking a `Link` that happens to have the same `href` as the current page causes the entire page to empty.

Here's an example:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6170607/154454937-1dc1c3db-5faf-48ee-8ab9-599157287ccb.mp4

This problem only seems to happen with React Server Components enabled.


### Expected Behavior

Clicking a `Link` that happens to have the same `href` as the current page should not cause the entire page to go empty.

Instead, it should behave exactly like without React Server Components enabled: You just continue to see the current page without any problems.

### To Reproduce

1. Clone the Vercel's [next-react-server-components](https://github.com/vercel/next-react-server-components) example
2. Add `<Link href="/rsc">This link points to the current page</Link>` in `pages/rsc.server.js`
3. Click the link and watch the whole page go empty

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

Despite consulting docs and trying various approaches I cannot set multiple outing going Set-Cookie headers from middleware. Multiple appended `Set-Cookie` headers will be folded into a single header string, delimited by a `,`.

- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/30560
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/30560/files

I think cloudflare still folds the headers, despite the vercel implementation. See demo below (especially the testing pane)
https://cloudflareworkers.com/?_gl=1*eoxc09*_ga*ODUyNTAyNTk3LjE2MzczMzgwNDc.*_gid*OTcyMTI5ODYxLjE2NDUwMDI0MDc.#a1316a97e1bfe87e7ac74b95032c55d1:https://vercel.com/

### Expected Behavior

I should see multiple `Set-Cookie` response headers.

### To Reproduce

You can reproduce it yourself easily enough. (using NextResponse.cookie just wraps the headers.append call, so using it directly for brevity)

```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";

export default async function middleware(_request: NextRequest) {
    const resp = NextResponse.next();
    resp.headers.append("Set-Cookie", "cookie1=val1");
    resp.headers.append("Set-Cookie", "cookie2=val2");
    return resp;
}
```

Once deployed to vercel you will see in the response that the headers are concatenated.

![Screenshot 2022-02-17 at 11 32 48](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/660635/154474009-7e3f0c73-51c0-40e2-975e-c6be9e40488c.png)
author: tavvy

title: Steaming ISR
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Recently with the release of [Next.js V12.1](https://nextjs.org/blog/next-12-1) came along on-demand ISR updating. It would be very nice if there was a streaming-based approach to ISR, meaning whenever the content is invalidated, the components using the data from the `getStaticProps` automatically re-render with the new data.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Ideally, no code changes should be required. This could be made a config option if need be. 

I see this being very useful in various use cases, for example, a Github Issues Client. My app currently uses ISR and now updates on-demand, but would be really nice if the component that renders the comments would update whenever the data is invalidated so that it is updated in real-time.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Just refresh the page, however, if you refresh the page while editing text, you will lose that progress. This is a very flawed workaround.

body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

    Operating System:
      Platform: win32
      Arch: x64
      Version: Windows 10 Pro
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.0
      npm: 8.1.0
      Yarn: 1.22.5
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.1.0
      react: 18.0.0-rc.0
      react-dom: 18.0.0-rc.0

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

Starting with `v12.1.0`, you can't use React 18 when you don't use the `experimental` field in the `next.config.js`

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29319414/154569017-38f72690-6879-47d1-a0cd-09072af2967c.png)

That's because [this recent change](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/commit/1aee93581f7154ca4c191388904c33980cd74653) sets `reactRoot` on the user's config without checking if the key already exists:

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/787186a85a054ea870fc964583fe65e9f2286354/packages/next/server/config.ts#L679-L682

### Expected Behavior

- React 18 should be usable without using the `experimental` field in the `next.config.js`
or
- the [docs for React 18](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/react-18/overview) should mention that it is mandatory to set the field

### To Reproduce

1. Install via Create Next App
`npx create-next-app@latest --ts --use-npm .`
2. Install React 18
`npm install next@latest react@rc react-dom@rc`
3. Start the app
`npm run dev`

author: dcschmid

title: Deprecate `page` parameter for Middleware
body:
Currently, in Middleware, `NextRequest` holds a `page` property that can be used to _foresee_ if the incoming request will match a page or not. This feature is intended to help users detect if a certain page will be render and, in the case of dynamic routes, which ones are the parameters that they will be matching.

This information is based on simply checking if there is a static/dynamic page route that matches the request. The problem is that when the request matches a static file, it will still check if there is a page match and it is not possible to detect if there is a static file that matches at that specific stage.

This feature can be replaced by using [path-to-regexp](https://github.com/pillarjs/path-to-regexp) or any other library or regexp and it is adding logic to the middleware bundle that many users may not need, growing its size when there is no need to.

We should entirely remove the feature so that when `request.page` is accessed we can fail with an error and a link that provides a code snippet with an alternative to the feature.

4. request `localhost:3000/rsc`

title: Support tsconfig paths without baseUrl
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Typescript supports `paths` [without specifying `baseUrl`](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/pull/40101). This is interesting when you want to use some path aliases without allowing bare imports to resolve from the project root.

### Describe the solution you'd like

next.js already supports `paths` with `baseUrl`. It could just follow what typescript does and resolve `paths` from the config location when `baseUrl` is not specified.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Configure `baseUrl`

title: Hard crash when applying suggested tsconfig.json in read-only environments
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

```
    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #1 SMP Wed Jun 16 23:47:55 UTC 2021
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.0
      npm: 8.1.0
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.11-canary.21
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

N/A

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

N/A

### Describe the Bug

In some build environments (CI, Bazel, ...) the source code may be mounted at a read-only path. If next attempts to modify the tsconfig.json file it'll crash out the build with an error such as:

```
[Error: EACCES: permission denied, open '<snip>/test-next/nextjs-blog/tsconfig.json'] {
  errno: -13,
  code: 'EACCES',
  syscall: 'open',
  path: '<snip>/test-next/nextjs-blog/tsconfig.json'
}
```

### Expected Behavior

Not crashing. I'm happy that Next has opinions and helpful onboarding, but the error message has too little info and isn't actionable. Also, I'd expect the process to continue as long as it's not a user request to create the file -- I'd happily have a long error log due to missing an option, and find a warning about tsconfig.json at the top than a random undiagnosable crash. 

### To Reproduce

1. Follow the get started tutorial up until you're able to `$ npm run dev`.
2. `$ touch pages/foobar.ts`
3. `$ npm install --save-dev typescript @types/react @types/node`
4. `$ npm run build` so next.js generates a `tsconfig.json`
5. Edit `tsconfig.json` and remove a compiler option -- I assume all trigger it, but I removed the `moduleResolution` option.
6. `$ chmod -w tsconfig.json`
7. `$ npm run build`

Crash here: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/lib/typescript/writeConfigurationDefaults.ts#L174. I assume it'll also crash here if it's first-time setup in a read-only environment: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/lib/typescript/writeConfigurationDefaults.ts#L98
author: tgolsson

title: Revalidate array of paths – On demand revalidate
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

It would be great if unstable_revalidate could accept an array of paths. 

A lot of time content is referenced on other pages. By only updating the main page via i.e. a webhook, stale content can be shown on other pages. 

I.e. updating the phone number of a contact person. We both want to update the /person/<name> but we also might want to update pages where the person is referenced such as /solutions/finance.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Allow array of paths in unstable_revalidate.

``await res.unstable_revalidate(['/person/john/', '/news/article-written-by-tom'])``

### Describe alternatives you've considered

````
// /api/revalidate.ts

 Promise.all(
    paths.map(async (path) => {
      await res.unstable_revalidate(path)
    })
  )
````

This should work, but is probably not very scaleable.
author: heggemsnes

title: routing behind reverse proxies (use x-forwarded-host)
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Our stack runs a next site in a docker container. We would like to be able to use domain routing for localization. Unfortunately, the `host` header is the URL of the container it's deployed on. We figured out that the `x-forwarded-host` wasn't set, so we did in our proxy. When I dug deeper in the code of how next and the i18n module does the routing, I found that it only looks at the `host` header.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I found https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/20662 which adds a config option to trust proxies. This would fix our problems with the headers. 
Or if anyone has a workaround to also include the `x-forwarded-host` in the `getHostname` functions, that could also help.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I haven't found any other alternative for our particular deployment.
https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/20662 would be perfect and I would be happy to make a new PR that also ads this to the i18n module.

title: Tree-shaking fails for different imports from the same file across multiple routes
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

```
> next info

/bin/sh: pnpm: command not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 19.6.0: Tue Oct 12 18:34:05 PDT 2021; root:xnu-6153.141.43~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 12.22.9
      npm: 8.3.1
      Yarn: 1.22.5
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.11-canary.21
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```


### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

When importing from the same file across multiple routes, the NextJS output bundle for those routes **includes all the code from the file that is used by any route, regardless of whether it is needed for a particular route**. The imported code is clearly tree-shakable / side-effect free. Note that unnecessary code is omitted from all routes correctly if _none_ of the routes import it.

### Expected Behavior

I expect the output bundle for a specific route to omit unnecessary code that is only used by a different route.

In the below example, the JS bundle for the `/test` route should not have "string2".

### To Reproduce

Example:
```ts
// strings.ts - shared utility file
export const STRING1 = "string1";
export const STRING2 = "string2";

// pages/test.tsx
import { STRING1 } from '../strings';
...

// pages/test2.tsx
import { STRING2 } from '../strings';
...

// -> JS bundle for /test route includes both "string1" and "string2" definitions
```

Minimal repro example repository: https://github.com/joyt/nextjs-tree-shake-fail

### Describe the feature you'd like to request

I just played with On-Demand Incremental Static Regeneration, made myself a demo also https://playing-revalidate.vercel.app/ but I think `revalidate` function should support taking in a list of url paths instead of just single url paths as I think we will have cases where you need to revalidate multiple pages instead of just one single ... Thanks and Love 💘

Currently 

```ts
unstable_revalidate: (urlPath: string) => Promise<void>;

```

What I am requesting

```ts
unstable_revalidate: (urlPaths: string | string[]) => Promise<void>;

```

title: Custom CSS Configuration Detection False Positives
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:54 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 17.4.0
      npm: 8.3.1
      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

The test that Next.js uses to detect custom css loaders is too simple. 

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/27affb414159bea10cca57b1291fe7037ee90bdb/packages/next/build/webpack-config.ts#L1850
``` js
const fileNames = [
      '/tmp/test.css',
      '/tmp/test.scss',
      '/tmp/test.sass',
      '/tmp/test.less',
      '/tmp/test.styl',
    ]
```

Loaders that search for the string `test` will cause a false positive -  due to the above list of test strings all containing `test`. While such loader will intentionally match `test.css`, it doesn't match other `.css` files, and so Next.js should not detect that custom CSS configuration has been added.

The string `test` is too simple, and it is reasonable that a loader might match on it.
A significantly longer, more complex string would avoid this behaviour: `NEXTJS_CUSTOM_CSS_DETECTION_FILE.xxx`?

### Expected Behavior

Next.js does not detect that custom CSS configuration has been added if loaders that search for the string `test` are present.

### To Reproduce

Add 
```
config.module.rules.push({
  test: /\.?test(\.\w+)?$/,

  ...

})
```
to webpack config.

Note how default CSS loaders are disabled with accompanying error message.

title: Shared components need to be able to import client components
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: arm64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.2.0: Sun Nov 28 20:28:41 PST 2021; root:xnu-8019.61.5~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000
    Binaries:
      Node: 17.5.0
      npm: 8.4.1
      Yarn: 1.22.15
      pnpm: 6.11.0
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.1.1-canary.1
      react: 0.0.0-experimental-13036bfbc-20220121
      react-dom: 0.0.0-experimental-13036bfbc-20220121

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

Importing a Client Component from a Shared (via Server) component runs it in the Server environment. But it should turn it into a Module Reference instead.

So `index.server.js` -> `components.js` -> `thing.client.js` tries to run `<Thing />` *in the SC environment*. But it should treat it as a client import, and "skip" over it.

https://github.com/gaearon/sc-bug-repro/blob/df693017248e13c2c59829a27aa7ff6b63aad341/pages/index.server.js#L3-L8

In other words, imports to Client files only seem to be replaced with Module References inside `.server.js` files. But they should be replaced with Module References in all files.

### Expected Behavior

It should work

### To Reproduce

```
git clone https://github.com/gaearon/sc-bug-repro.git
cd sc-bug-repro

git checkout shared-client

npm install
npm run dev
```

See `pages/index.server.js` for two snippets that should be equivalent but aren't.

body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Currently in next/image, we need to all subdomains for all external URLs to allow fetching images from them. This could get problematic when we use multiple subdomains from the same domain. As the number of subdomains grow, it becomes difficult maintain.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Solution 1. We can add another array attribute to `images` config, called `allowSubdomainsFrom`.  Then, next/image would support all the domains mentioned inside that array.

Solution 2. We can allow all subdomains for the `domains` given in the images config.

author: to-long

title: Allow inline-scripts with `beforeInteractive` strategy
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

My library [`next-themes`](https://github.com/pacocoursey/next-themes) injects an inline script that **must** at the start of page load to avoid any color flashing. This means I must use the `beforeInteractive` strategy in `next/script`.

However, `beforeInteractive` does not support inline scripts:

![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34928425/154853949-db4873cd-12c7-4f79-8306-157cb8773a50.png)

**I'd like to request that inline scripts should be supported in `next/script` with `strategy="beforeInteractive"`.**

### Describe the solution you'd like

Allow passing `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` or `children` alongside usages of the script component with `strategy="beforeInteractive"`.

Implementation strategy:
1. Pass `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` or `children` to `scripts.beforeInteractive` [on this line](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/bf8b835e2e1fd4b8364d7411d630d139478e8ea5/packages/next/client/script.tsx#L157)
2. It should automatically be [passed to the rendered script tag when it's inserted via the `_document`](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/bf8b835e2e1fd4b8364d7411d630d139478e8ea5/packages/next/pages/_document.tsx#L82)

### Describe alternatives you've considered

1. **Use an external script**. Not possible because the value of my inline script is constructed at runtime based on the Next.js page props.
2. **Base64 encoding the script as the `src` prop**. This is what I'm currently doing, but it means we cannot have a stable, secure way to allow the script via CSP.

author: parlay96

title: Manual revalidate ISG would have support for I18N and dynamic routes
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Hi NextJS team.

Firstly thanks for on-demand (manually) revalidation support for statically pre-rendered (getStaticProps) pages. You guys already know that we wanted and waited for this feature in hard way really as community.

After release of latest version I tried it immediately with `res.unstable_revalidate ` api. It works in smooth way. But I realized that it works only for provided url directly. I mean let's say I have a blog and I am supporting `en` and `es` languages.

I have `posts/[id]` page structure. Let's say on this post page I am also fetching and showing my total posts count from an external API.
When I add a new post I want to update all old ones with a web-hook. When I trigger revalidate as like `await res.unstable_revalidate('/en/posts/12')` , it revalidates for only post page of with ID 12 and only English version.

It would be really great if I can revalidate for all versions of that page. The I18N part is not hard. If I am supporting for example 4 languages I can loop them and trigger 4 different `res.unstable_revalidate` requests. It is OK.

But the dynamic urls are not that easy. Because you know if I use ISG I am providing some URLs with getStaticPaths as initially but some URLs being prepared on runtime with ISG. I can't know which pages generated on runtime so I can not trigger revalidate request for that pages.

### Describe the solution you'd like

There can be a custom url pattern in `res.unstable_revalidate` API for dynamic url pages.
For example: `await res.unstable_revalidate('/en/posts/*')`  The asterisk character means purge (revalidate) all routes based on `/en/posts/` .

Also it can take an options argument and it includes a flag for purging all I18N routes for that url.

i.e.: `await res.unstable_revalidate('/posts/*', {revalidateAllI18NVersions: true})` 

Now it will revalidate all I18N versions (en, es) and all paths (/posts/1, /posts/2, /posts/3..... all getStaticPaths + generated paths on runtime with ISG).


### Describe alternatives you've considered

Maybe you guys can expose `incrementalCache` to `req.context` on API handler. So, we can revalidate some pages manually in two steps:

1. Remove static generated pages from .next/server/pages
2. Purge `incrementalCache` for related urls.

I am currently doing this but this requires creating a custom server and it makes more complex to handling things. (You can check from here: https://github.com/MetinArslanturk/microfrontend-nextjs-ssr/blob/i18n-integration/next-host/scripts/utils.js ) Handling from API routes would be easier.

Also handling it with directly the new `unstable_revalidate `API would be much much easier.

If you guys check example repo (in i18n-integration branch), You will see why we need this. We are using dynamic microfrontend pieces on pages. When a microfrontend app has new deployment we want to revalidate all related pages.

Thanks from now,
Metin
author: MetinArslanturk

title: Preflight HEAD Request for Pages with Middlewares
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.5.0: Sat May  8 05:10:33 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.121.3~9/RELEASE_X86_64
Binaries:
      Node: 14.17.3
      npm: 6.14.13
      Yarn: 1.22.11
Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

For each client-side navigation to a page that has a middleware associated with it, Next.js makes a `HEAD` request before the actual chunks are loaded and API Calls are processed from the `getInitialProps` or `getServerSideProps` blocks. This HEAD request is part of the preflight requests made by the Next.js router.

https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/eddabd98f8e4e5a82f4341bc1d8926959327919a/packages/next/shared/lib/router/router.ts#L1973-L1994

The issue is that this request is extremely slow and blocks all processing while it happens, i.e: Chunk loading and all other page calls. If I remove the middleware the HEAD Call goes away and the navigation is performant again, question is: **Is this call required? And is there a way for it to be more optimized/faster?**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35926159/154973253-3b06e656-470f-466d-ab65-146c068962c1.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35926159/154973282-fc0fa612-5c73-4065-ab78-600ddb726a79.png)


### Expected Behavior

The HEAD request isn't adding anything to the page in terms of middleware since Middlewares don't run on the client-side, can the HEAD Call be removed on the client-side or at least made to run in parallel with the page-chunk load and other API Calls In `getInitialProps` or `getServerSideProps` or made more performant?

### To Reproduce

In order to reproduce this issue, use the following steps:

- Setup a Next.js project.
- Create a few pages like `/`, `/page1`, `/page2` with placeholder/empty `getInitialProps` blocks.
- Setup a Middleware at the root that simply forwards the request using `NextResponse.next()`.
- Setup navigation between the pages using `next/link` in a navbar and put it at the top using `_app.jsx`.
- Try client-side navigating between the pages, a `HEAD` call should be present before each page starts loading.

title: NextJS hot reload leaks memory
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

```

$ next info
'pnpm' is not recognized as an internal or external command,
operable program or batch file.

    Operating System:        
      Platform: win32        
      Arch: x64
      Version: Windows 10 Pro
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.14.0
      npm: 8.3.1
      Yarn: 1.22.11
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:       
      next: 12.1.1-canary.1  
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2    

```

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

98.0.4758.102

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next dev

### Describe the Bug

Hey,
It seems that nextjs leak all previous version of hot reload, even when they are old and not used:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/76743257/155036275-9e0d3189-6e3a-40cf-a1e4-9d30d34170ce.png)
as you can see from the picture `_app.js` `_document.js` are duplicated for each of their revision, even when not used at all, and even when closing the browser tab. It causes memory to go up until everything crashes on OOM error with some large projects. 

### Expected Behavior

Only one instance of each page/code should be kept in memory, otherwise this leak cause crash. 

### To Reproduce

https://github.com/smulikHakipod/nextjs-hot-reload-leak

Start NextJS in dev mode e.g `npm run dev`
Go to some page e.g `index` e.g `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`
Change a char in `_app.js` and save it, causing a hot reload.
Refresh the page (index) in the browser.
Repeat.
Watch memory grow and heap snapshot containing all duplicated pages content.
Repeat enough time, and it will crash or run out of system memory.

Thanks!!

> Loading .env (and all variants) into process.env

`.env` will be loaded, but `.env.development` will not.

### Expected Behavior

`.env.development` will also be loaded.

### To Reproduce

1. Fork [official example](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/examples/with-jest)
2. Add .env.development
3. Add tests that depend on environment variables
4. Run tests

title: Documented use of Google Fonts via next/head generates a warning in development mode
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

```
    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.3.0: Wed Jan  5 21:37:58 PST 2022; root:xnu-8019.80.24~20/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000
    Binaries:
      Node: 14.18.1
      npm: 6.14.15
      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.1.0
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next start

### Describe the Bug

When a Google Fonts stylesheet is included in a page by following the [font optimization documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/font-optimization), a warning is generated in the browser console in development mode: `Do not add stylesheets using next/head (see <link rel="stylesheet"> tag`.



### Expected Behavior

I would expect any of:

* an exemption in the code for Google Fonts or TypeKit here, similar to the font optimization trigger in [next/head](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/d7de7deb70e58de84db16d8ae04010f306ec0d32/packages/next/shared/lib/head.tsx#L146-L152)
* the [font optimization documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/font-optimization) to omit usage on a page and only describe its use in `Document`
* the ability to disable the warning in the browser console

(For my use case, never warning on hosted fonts and/or ignoring the warning would be ideal, as I'd like to be able to allow different fonts on a per-page basis, and because the failure cases are unlikely to apply.)

### To Reproduce

Include 
```tsx
      <Head>
        <link
          href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter&display=optional"
          rel="stylesheet"
        />
      </Head>
``` 
in a page in development mode. Observe the warning in the console.

npm run dev

### Describe the Bug

Hello and thanks again for merging my 2nd pull request!

Currently I have a strange but annoying issue, not sure if it's only browser related.  When I use onLoadingComplete I get wrong naturalWidth and naturalHeight properties, they are both zero or one. That happens currently ONLY in FIrefox and ONLY when the image loads lazily e.g. when scrolling to its position. Adding "priority" prop or switching browser, the issue dissapears. Any ideas?
I need  those numbers to be correct in order to calibrate the parent container with the correct aspect ratio for resizing purposes.

OnLoading complete should report correct naturalHeight and naturalwIdth in every case.

### To Reproduce

<Image   
        src={imageLocation}        
        onLoadingComplete={(e)=>console.log(e.naturalHeight)}
        layout="fill"             
       
      />

author: Jonty800

title: Can `create-next-app`'s default typescript extend one of the default bases from TypeScript?
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

When you run `create-next-app`, you get the following for the `tsconfig.json`:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "es5",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "node",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```
While this is fine, it does put a decent amount on the end user to understand if they were to enter this file.

### Describe the solution you'd like

TypeScript's docs specify [default bases for typescript configs](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/tsconfig-json.html#tsconfig-bases).

Main benefits:
- standardizes between those using `create-next-app` and those using a bespoke, custom configuration
- centralizes the standards and best practices for the default next configuration

```json
{
  "extends": "@tsconfig/next/tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    // if they override, they override here.
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

The current solution is working, this is just an enhancement.

title: Hash change re-renders the entire app, _app, page and components involved
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.3.0: Wed Jan  5 21:37:58 PST 2022; root:xnu-8019.80.24~20/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.0
      npm: 8.1.0
      Yarn: 3.2.0
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.1.0
      react: 18.0.0-rc.0
      react-dom: 18.0.0-rc.0

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

Chrome 101.0.4899.0 same in FF and Safari

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next dev, next start

### Describe the Bug

Navigation to anchors should not cause any re-render; however, changing the hash through NextJs building blocks Link and Router does cause a re-render.

Re-rendering an entire app from _app and the respective page with the page's content can get quite substantial. 

E.g., in our case, MDX and quite large React components get re-rendered. On the other hand, the re-rendering leads us to identify a hard-to-track problem in loading MDX components, but this is off-topic to the current issue.

Looking at the sources, it seems the function of the router does not lead to a state change, which causes a re-rendering because the operations target Html primitives primarily. Logging the hashChangeStart and hashChangeComplete events seems something is causing a re-render afterward; maybe a new page-props object gets created, but this is just a vague assumption. 

Another thing we see is when triggering an identical hash change several times using NextJs Link and Router primitives; the re-rendering occurs multiple times, one for each interaction. In this case, the browser history seems to be intact; a second click on the same link does not cause a push of another state to browser history; the browser's back button works; in any case, a re-render of the app gets executed.

### Expected Behavior

Do not perform re-rendering if it is not needed.

### To Reproduce

The sources of the reproducer are here. https://github.com/gurkerl83/NextJs-Hash-Bug

1. Clone the repo
2. Install dependencies
- yarn
3. Run development respectively production
- yarn dev
- yarn start
4. Follow the instructions in the apps index page and see the console / http://localhost:3000

The link to the deployed version
https://next-js-hash-bug.vercel.app

Thx!

title: Encoded slashes not working as expected in production in an API catch-all route
body:
### Verify canary release

- [x] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

Operating System:
  Platform: linux
  Arch: x64
  Version: #1 SMP PREEMPT Sun Feb 6 12:21:42 UTC 2022
Binaries:
  Node: 16.13.2
  npm: 8.4.1
  Yarn: 1.22.15
  pnpm: 6.11.0
Relevant packages:
  next: 12.1.1-canary.2
  react: 17.0.2
  react-dom: 17.0.2

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next build && next start on Vercel

### Describe the Bug

My application is using an API catch-all route which tests that the slug length is 3 or less and returns 404 not found otherwise. The last slug part has an arbitrary content, including another slashes, and can be encoded using encodeURIComponent so it doesn't get to conflict with the catch-all filter.
Locally, everything works correctly, but in production (Vercel) breaks when using slashes, as reported in TheDavidDelta/lingva-translate#68. Indeed, I've already created a similar issue in the past but for standard client catch-all routes (#24775). That issue was succesfully solved, but the problem persisted on the API routes, as reported in the linked issue of my repo. I re-opened the issue but it got no attention and got closed for inactivity.
Recently, I've just updated to Next 12.1 and the problem is still there, [as you can see in this link](https://lingva.ml/api/v1/en/fr/test%2Ftest), so I just created a new issue in order to solve it.

### Expected Behavior

To don't break nor send 404 when using encoded slashes on filtered catch-all routes, as works locally.

### To Reproduce

Go to https://lingva.ml/ and write any translation that contains a slash.

title: [NEXT-781] strict mode error for projects that do not specify `"type": "module"`
body:
### Verify canary release - [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release ### Provide environment information Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: arm64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Tue Oct 12 18:33:38 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.8~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T8101 Binaries: Node: 16.13.1 npm: 8.1.2 Yarn: N/A pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.1.0 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ### What browser are you using? (if relevant) Chrome ### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant) _No response_ ### Describe the Bug In `next dev`, Non-ESM files default to `strict mode` in projects that do not specify `"type": "module"` in package.json Therefore, the non-ESM script is trapped by the strict mode validation and an error occurs. ```js // non-esm.js with (Math) { console.log(PI); } ``` ```ts // pages/index.tsx import type { NextPage } from "next"; import { useEffect } from "react"; const Home: NextPage = () => { useEffect(() => { require("../non-esm.js"); // Error!! }, []); return
test
; }; export default Home; ``` babel error: swc error: In the case of babel-loader, this problem can be avoided by changing the extension from `js` to `cjs`. However, for swc, this problem cannot be avoided with either extension. This problem occurs at `next@12.1.0`. Possible causes of PR: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/33637 ### Expected Behavior `next dev` works without error.　 ### To Reproduce - `npx create-next-app@latest` - `npm run dev` - Go to http://localhost:3000/ [NEXT-781](https://linear.app/vercel/issue/NEXT-781/strict-mode-error-for-projects-that-do-not-specify-type-module)

title: router.asPath contains dynamic path as query params in non default language on vercel.
body: ### Verify canary release - [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release ### Provide environment information ``` Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: x64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.1.0: Wed Oct 13 17:33:01 PDT 2021; root:xnu-8019.41.5~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 Binaries: Node: 14.18.1 npm: 6.14.15 Yarn: 1.22.17 pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.1.1-canary.2 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ``` ### What browser are you using? (if relevant) _No response_ ### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant) Vercel ### Describe the Bug `router.asPath` contains dynamic path as query params in non default language on vercel. Example - Assume there is a page `[...search].js`. For path `/my-path`, `router.asPath` returns `/my-path` on server and `/my-path` on client. For path `/nl/my-path`, `router.asPath` returns `/nl/my-path?search=my-path` on server and `/my-path` on client. Only happens for non-default locale. Only happens on vercel. ### Expected Behavior For path `/nl/my-path`, `router.asPath` returns `/my-path` ### To Reproduce See the implementation here. https://github.com/akshitkrnagpal/vercel-as-path-issue 1. Go to page https://vercel-as-path-issue.vercel.app/nl/my-path 2. You will see `asPath` as `/my-path` 3. Do view source. You will see different `asPath` in html. See image below.

body: ### Verify canary release - [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release ### Provide environment information 'yarn' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. 'pnpm' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. Operating System: Platform: win32 Arch: x64 Version: Windows 10 Home Binaries: Node: 14.18.1 npm: 7.20.3 Yarn: N/A pnpm: N/A Relevant packages: next: 12.1.0 react: 17.0.2 react-dom: 17.0.2 ### What browser are you using? (if relevant) All main web browsers except Safari (I don't have Safari). ### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant) _No response_ ### Describe the Bug I have a component for animited photos and I use . When this one show animated photo, I have static one, but when I change on , the files are animited (e. g. eyes are moving). For example: https://pronama.jp/images/animated.png ### Expected Behavior When Image get animited files (apng, gif or animited webp and avif), Image should know this isn't static photo, yet animited one, so files should move. That files should be converting to avif or webp when web browser support that formats, especially gif format. Gif format must be converted to avif, webp or apng format by the performance of that format. ### To Reproduce I change to img tag: it work. I added "unoptimized": it work too. I added Image without unoptimized: it doesn't work. title: Docs: clarification on production builds into docker and bundling dependencies
body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

I have a next app in a monorepo context which depends on 2 other apps (or packages). All of them need to run in parallel which I currently achieve with the monorepo tool I'm using (Lerna). I currently find myself needing to the copy the full `node_modules` folder of the next app into my production docker image which I want to run on AWS, otherwise the app wont run. 

For obvious reasons I would prefer to only bundle the absolute minimum assets to run my application, so only built outputs and needed dependencies, while discarding devDependencies since they are irrelevant and increase size. I cannot find docs on this. [As mentioned in this other ticket asking the same thing](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/5531):

> I ask because, when building for Docker, I would like to create the smallest image possible, and when I add a framework (such as material-ui) I am accustomed to adding it as a devDependency. However, with NextJS, it is unclear to me what the runtime needs are: does NextJS need such dependencies for SSR, etc.?

### Is there any context that might help us understand?

I have found 2 similar questions that were closed and point to a discussion that does not seem to offer a solution or information about this request:
* https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/5531
* https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/5432
* https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/4496#issuecomment-512413936 (main issue they point to, which seems like a different use case)

My Next version is 11.

### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

_No response_

title: Unable to opt out of builtin polyfills
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

Next: 10.2.3
Node: 14.15.4

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

Chrome 65

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

_No response_

### Describe the Bug

Application needs to support older versions of Chrome, in this example v60-68. The app uses a video player package built on jQuery 3.4. The video player uses the method `$.replaceWith()` or `$.replaceAll()` which is where the error occurs with the Next.js `array.flat` polyfill.

```
  var flat = arr.flat ? function(array) {
    return arr.flat.call(array); // Next's polyfill errors here and needs to be disabled so jQuery can fall through
  } : function(array) {
    return arr.concat.apply([], array); 
  }
```

If the [Next.js array.flat polyfill](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/0af3b526408bae26d6b3f8cab75c4229998bf7cb/packages/next-polyfill-module/src/index.js#L53) is removed and the `polyfill-module.js` file is rebuilt the error is no longer present.

Additionally if the Next polyfill is replaced with [`array.flat` polyfill from Array Flat Polyfill](https://github.com/jonathantneal/array-flat-polyfill/blob/master/src/polyfill-flat.js) the error also does not occur.

The bug in this instance is the default solution introduces conflicts with other popular libraries with no obvious way to gracefully opt out.


### Expected Behavior

Ideally would like to have Next default array.flat polyfill  work in all situations. Or at the very least a way to disable the provided polyfills through a `next.config` flag such as `useDefaultPolyfills: boolean`.

### To Reproduce

In Chrome v65

1. Set debugger before using `.replaceWith()`
![Screen Shot 2022-02-25 at 1 02 35 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3144419/155803022-e3326587-05f6-4c2d-be50-7ac81132eae5.png)

2. Stepping through till jQuery calling `flat(args)`
![Screen Shot 2022-02-25 at 1 03 21 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3144419/155803086-f625b541-1abb-4373-ba80-8acdaa9a25e9.png)

3. Calls `arr.flat.call(array)` because of the Next polyfill. (Will work if polyfill is removed from `polyfill-module.js`)
![Screen Shot 2022-02-25 at 1 03 30 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3144419/155803127-eb8228ef-f7c4-49b9-b070-b3cf8042b69a.png)

4. Fails with provided Next.js polyfill for `array.flat`
![Screen Shot 2022-02-25 at 1 05 49 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3144419/155803295-35d49033-2d3a-4f83-ba7a-a9d13d1a5dc7.png)
author: wsfuller

title: Support multiple callbacks for `router.beforePopState()`
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Currently, `router.beforePopState()` only supports [a single callback function](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/v12.1.1-canary.3/packages/next/shared/lib/router/router.ts#L1622). Subsequent calls to that function will wipe out any previously supplied callback functions. Perhaps this is intentional, but I'd prefer it to support a variable number of callbacks. I think this would be both functionally and intuitively superior.

My use case is simply that multiple components currently active in the hierarchy may wish to supply their own callback.

### Describe the solution you'd like

1. `beforePopState()` should simply push the supplied callback on to an array
2. It would return a cleanup function so that callees can remove the callback when unmounting (e.g. as a return value from a `useEffect` hook
3. If _any_ of the supplied callbacks return `false`, then the state change would be prevented, thus continuing to support the current ability to prevent a state change.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I've currently resorted to providing a proxied version of the router via context in my app so that I can augment the functionality in this way.

title: Next.js throws an exception when running in 3rd party iframe
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

Running the suggested commands failed to produce an output in the sandbox I have created to reproduce the issue. However, perhaps the relevant parameters can be looked up in the sandbox itself:

https://codesandbox.io/s/young-https-mdcctx

It's a clean template running the latest version of Next.js. 

### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

Chrome (latest version) - 3rd party cookies disabled

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next dev

### Describe the Bug

My example reproduces the behaviour when running Next.js in a 3rd party iframe - merely running a basic Next.js app in such a setup throws an exception (see console).

Demo website - https://rightsize-store.000webhostapp.com/next_js_test.html
Sandbox - https://codesandbox.io/s/young-https-mdcctx

Here's the error:

```
Error was not caught DOMException: Failed to read the 'localStorage' property from 'Window': Access is denied for this document.
    at initializeBuildWatcher (webpack-internal:///./node_modules/next/dist/client/dev/prerender-indicator.js:47:38)
    at eval (webpack-internal:///./node_modules/next/dist/client/next-dev.js:96:40)
```

In my actual application (too complex to easily reproduce in a sandbox) Next.js is throwing a similar exception:

```
Uncaught (in promise) DOMException: Failed to read the 'sessionStorage' property from 'Window': Access is denied for this document.
    at clearFullRefreshStorage (webpack-internal:///./node_modules/next/dist/client/dev/error-overlay/hot-dev-client.js:314:3)
    at handleApplyUpdates (webpack-internal:///./node_modules/next/dist/client/dev/error-overlay/hot-dev-client.js:265:5)
    at eval (webpack-internal:///./node_modules/next/dist/client/dev/error-overlay/hot-dev-client.js:287:5)
```

Looking into the usage of `clearFullRefreshStorage` inside internal Next.js files I can see that `sessionStorage` is manipulated not wrapped in a try/catch block. Is that intentional?

### Expected Behavior

I would expect that Next.js would work in all environments, including increasingly more restrictive of accessing cookies and web storage.

### To Reproduce

Simply visit https://rightsize-store.000webhostapp.com/next_js_test.html and look at console logs.
